body:
## What

`gbrain doctor` prints a `[doctor-categories] unknown check name … defaulting to 'meta'` stderr warning on **every run** — once for each of 7 checks. This categorizes them and closes the gap that let them slip through CI.

## Root cause

`src/commands/doctor.ts` merges `runAllOnboardChecks()` output into the report and categorizes each via `categorizeCheck(c.name)`. But those 7 checks are defined in `src/core/onboard/checks.ts`, and their names were never added to the category sets in `src/core/doctor-categories.ts` — so they fell through to `'meta'` with a warning, and were scored under the wrong category. The drift-guard test only scanned `doctor.ts`, so CI never noticed.

## Change

- Categorize the 7 onboard checks:
  - **brain** (data-health): `embed_staleness`, `entity_link_coverage`, `timeline_coverage`, `takes_count`
  - **meta** (gbrain coherence): `pack_upgrade_available`, `type_proliferation`, `dangling_aliases`
- Extend `test/doctor-categories.test.ts` to also scan `src/core/onboard/checks.ts`, so onboard checks are covered by the drift guard and a future uncategorized onboard check **fails the build instead of warning silently in production**.

## Test plan

- `bun run typecheck` — clean
- `bun test test/doctor-categories.test.ts` — 8 pass, 0 fail
- `bun test` doctor-category-dependent files (brain-checks-score, cause-rank, hidden-by-search-policy) — 39 pass, 0 fail
- `gbrain doctor` — the 7 `unknown check name` lines are gone (0 remaining)

Pre-existing, unrelated `.serial.test.ts` failures in the local environment (worker-registry, brain-durability, brain-registry, doctor-cli-smoke, hybrid-meta, migration) were confirmed identical on clean `master` with these changes stashed — not introduced by this PR (they stem from a live local brain + `OPENAI_API_KEY` set in the shell).

body:
## Summary

Closes #2349 / OPS-85.

`gbrain dream` now threads the resolved cycle source into the `orphans` phase, so orphan counts and page totals are computed against the same source scope as the cycle. This fixes inflated dream output where `orphans` reported brain-wide orphan/page counts while the user was looking at one source.

## What changed

- `runPhaseOrphans` accepts the resolved cycle `sourceId` and passes it to `findOrphans`.
- Orphans phase details include `source_id` when scoped, plus the `total_linkable` denominator already produced by the command path.
- Autopilot global maintenance passes `forceGlobalOrphans: true` so its `GLOBAL_PHASES` run stays brain-wide even when `repoPath` maps to a source.
- Cycle and autopilot regression tests cover explicit source scope, path-derived source scope, and the forced brain-wide maintenance path.

## Risk

Low. The fallback remains unscoped when no source can be resolved, preserving legacy behavior. `orphans` stays in the serialized global lane for scheduling; normal dream cycles scope the reported candidate set when a cycle source exists, while autopilot global maintenance explicitly keeps brain-wide behavior.

## Test plan

- [x] `bun test test/core/cycle.serial.test.ts`
- [x] `bun test test/orphans.test.ts test/orphans-source-scope.test.ts test/orphans-pure-fn.test.ts`

- [x] Full verify dry-list run sequentially via `bash scripts/run-verify-parallel.sh --dry-list` because this macOS environment lacks `timeout`/`gtimeout` and the parallel runner misreports successful checks as rc=143.

body:
## Problem

The `sync.repo` and `extract.all` remediations (and `embed.stale`'s sync prerequisite) gate on `health.stale_pages`, defined in both engines as:

```sql
count(*) FROM pages p WHERE p.updated_at < (SELECT MAX(te.created_at) FROM timeline_entries te WHERE te.page_id = p.id)
```

i.e. "pages whose content `updated_at` predates their newest timeline entry." That was a meaningful proxy only while a trigger bumped `pages.updated_at` on every timeline insert — **a trigger dropped in migration v10** (`drop_timeline_search_trigger`). Since then the metric reflects a near-never condition, yet it still fires the two recommendations with the rationale **"N stale pages on disk"** — which is not what it measures and disagrees with both `gbrain sync` (commit-clean) and `doctor`'s `links_extraction_lag`. Operators see sync/extract recs that don't map to real work.

## Fix — gate on the real extraction lag

- `RecommendationContext` gains **`extractionLagPages`**, populated by `loadRecommendationContext` from **`engine.countStalePagesForExtraction`** — the *same* counter the `gbrain extract --stale` walk processes and doctor's `links_extraction_lag` check reports. So the recommendation fires **iff running `extract` will actually reduce it**, and clears once extraction is current. No re-implemented predicate → no drift between the recommender and the job it recommends.
- `sync.repo` / `extract.all` / `embed.stale`'s sync-dependency now gate on `extractionLagPages`; rationale text is honest (`"N page(s) with stale link/timeline edges"`). The sync→extract pipeline shape is unchanged (sync is the prerequisite that brings pages current before extract materializes their edges).
- `health.stale_pages` is **retained** (CLI health line + back-compat — no API/type break) but marked **legacy** in its docstring and **no longer gates any recommendation**.

## Testing

- `test/brain-score-recommendations.test.ts` updated to drive the gate via `ctx.extractionLagPages` (the pure-function pins for `sync.repo`/`extract.all`/`embed.stale` dependencies + severity ordering + determinism).
- `test/remediation-context-extraction-lag.test.ts` (new): PGLite engine test pins that `loadRecommendationContext` reports 0 on an empty brain and >0 once a page needs extraction.
- `bun run typecheck` clean · `bash scripts/run-verify-parallel.sh` 30/30 · recommender + onboard + advisor/features suites green (0 fail).

Companion to #2361 (terminate the `onboard --auto` loop) and #2362 (capability/content gates) — together they make `onboard --auto` recommend only work that can actually clear. Versioning/CHANGELOG left to the maintainer's ship.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: fix(remediation): gate sync/extract recs on real extraction lag, not the stale_pages proxy

body:
## Problem

`gbrain onboard --check --explain` keeps surfacing remediations that can **never clear** on a given brain, because they're emitted purely on a coverage threshold with no check that the recommended job is *capable* of moving that metric:

- **`extract-ner`** is recommended whenever `entity_link_coverage` is low — even when the active schema pack declares no `link_types[].inference.regex` rules. `extractNerLinks` then early-returns `pack_unavailable` and creates 0 links, so coverage never moves and the rec re-fires forever. (Reproduces on `gbrain-base`, which has no NER inference rules.)
- **`extract-timeline-from-meetings`** is recommended whenever `timeline_coverage` is low — even when there are **no dated meeting pages** to extract from. The handler skips meetings without `effective_date`, so it creates 0 entries and the rec persists.

Net effect: `onboard --check` nags with auto-eligible recommendations that are structural no-ops, even when `doctor` is otherwise green. An operator (or an automated remediation agent) runs the direct equivalents, sees them no-op, and the recommendations stay — confusing, and it makes `onboard --auto` burn cycles on work that can't help.

## Fix — gate each rec on whether the action can do anything

- **`packSupportsNerInference(pack)`** — extracted into `src/core/schema-pack/best-effort.ts` (co-located with the pack loader). It's the exact capability `extractNerLinks` already checks (`some link_type with inference.regex`). The handler now calls it too, so **the recommender and the handler gate on one definition and can't drift** — recommending a fix the handler will silently skip is precisely the bug this closes.
- **Timeline gate** — `checkTimelineCoverage` only emits the rec when `COUNT(*) FROM pages WHERE type='meeting' AND effective_date IS NOT NULL > 0`, matching the handler's own precondition.

When a gate withholds the rec, the check **stays WARN** (coverage really is low — honest, and `doctor`'s exit-code contract is unchanged) and the message explains *why* there's no auto-fix (e.g. "the active schema pack declares no NER inference rules"). The merged coverage branches preserve the original severity/est_seconds tiers via ternaries.

## Testing

- `test/onboard-phantom-rec-gates.test.ts` (new): pure unit tests pin `packSupportsNerInference` (null / no link_types / inference-without-regex / regex present); PGLite engine tests pin both gates — withheld-with-reason when the capability/content is absent, and emitted when a dated meeting exists.
- `bun run typecheck` clean.
- `bash scripts/run-verify-parallel.sh` — 30/30 green.
- Onboard suites (`onboard-phantom-rec-gates`, `onboard-pack-upgrade-checks`, `onboard-full-flow`) — 28 pass / 0 fail. The affected `extract-ner` path passes in isolation.

Companion to #2361 (which terminated the `onboard --auto` *loop*); this stops the loop from being *fed* un-satisfiable recommendations in the first place. Versioning/CHANGELOG left to the maintainer's ship.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: fix(onboard): gate extract-ner / timeline recs on whether the action can actually help

body:
## Problem

`gbrain onboard --auto` (and `doctor --remediate`) can spin forever, making no forward progress, when a remediation step's job reaches a terminal **non-completed** state — most easily reproduced by **cancelling** an in-flight job mid-run, but the same trap fires for any `failed`/`dead` step whose underlying health metric never improves.

## Root cause

The orchestrator loop (`runRemediation`, `src/core/remediation/run.ts`) does a per-step recheck (D7): after each step it recomputes recommendations from a fresh health snapshot and re-introduces any recommendation that still fires. A step that terminally failed is recorded in `abortedIds`, but the recompute **did not exclude it**:

```ts
recs = computeRecommendations(freshHealth, ctx).filter((r) => r.status === 'remediable');
```

Because idempotency keys are content-stable (same `job`+`params` → same key) and `queue.add` is already called with `max_attempts: 2`, re-dispatching an aborted step hits the queue's exactly-once fast path and returns the **same terminal row** every iteration. `waitForCompletion` returns it immediately, the step is re-aborted, the recompute re-emits it… and with `maxJobs` defaulting to `Infinity` the loop never terminates.

## Fix (caller-side; queue contract untouched)

The `idempotency_key` comment always said replay is *"caller responsibility — handled by the --remediate loop."* So the fix lives in the loop, not the shared primitive:

- **`selectActiveRecs(recs, abortedIds)`** (new, pure, in `remediation-step.ts`) — drops non-remediable steps **and** ids already in `abortedIds`. Used at the D7 recompute so a terminally-failed step is attempted **at most once per run**, then abandoned until the next scheduled run (which starts with a fresh abort set). This makes loop progress monotonic.
- **Top-of-loop guard** (`skipped_already_aborted`) — an explicit, locally-checkable backstop.
- **Queue idempotency left as-is.** `minions/queue.ts`'s exactly-once contract (unique partial index + `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`, with the deliberate "re-submit must not mutate the existing row" note) is intentional and shared by every caller (autopilot fan-out, crons, …). Changing it would be the wrong layer with a wide blast radius. The `:r<N>` retry-suffix replay referenced by several stale comments was **never implemented** and is unnecessary given queue-level `max_attempts`; those comments are updated to match reality.

## Testing

- `test/remediation-loop-termination.test.ts` (new) pins the termination invariant at the decision boundary: a re-emitted aborted step is filtered out so the active set strictly shrinks. (Full-orchestrator e2e with real Minion handlers stays deferred upstream per the existing TODO in `test/e2e/onboard-full-flow.test.ts` — the per-handler stub seam isn't landed yet.)
- `bun run typecheck` clean.
- `bash scripts/run-verify-parallel.sh` — 30/30 checks green (incl. `check:no-double-retry`).
- `bun test` on `remediation-step`, `brain-score-recommendations`, `doctor`, `minions`, `onboard-full-flow` + the new test — all pass (0 fail).

Versioning / CHANGELOG left to the maintainer's ship flow (community contribution).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: fix(remediation): terminate onboard --auto loop on a terminally-failed step

body:
## What

`extract-conversation-facts` (and the `parseConversation` orchestrator it uses)
silently extracted **nothing** from Slack-collector pages on brains that store
chat in the collector's native shapes. Two independent gaps stacked:

1. **Type routing.** The command's page-type allowlist is an exact match against
   `{conversation, meeting, slack, email}` (`extract-conversation-facts.ts`),
   passed straight to `listPages({ type })`. A brain that hasn't run the v2
   type-consolidation pack still carries the collector's granular types
   (`slack-dm-day`, `slack-thread`, `email-digest`), so `--types slack` matched
   **zero rows** and the whole comms corpus was skipped.

2. **Block-format parsing.** Even once routed, the 14 built-in patterns
   (`builtins.ts`) are all single-line (`**Name** (time): body`). The Slack
   collector emits a HEADER + indented-body BLOCK:

   ```
   - **Theo** (Mon 11:18)
     Hey everyone — quick update.

     Second paragraph of the same message.
   ```

   None of the 14 match (leading `- `, day-of-week + time with no trailing
   colon, body on the following lines), so `parseConversation` returned
   `phase: 'no_match'`, 0 messages — and the LLM fallback that would catch
   unknown formats is not yet wired (`parse.ts`). Net: no segments → no facts.

## How

- **`ALLOWED_TYPE_ALIASES` + `pageTypesForAllowed()`** in
  `extract-conversation-facts.ts` expand each logical type to the concrete
  `pages.type` values to enumerate (`slack` → `slack`, `slack-dm-day`,
  `slack-thread`; `email` → `email`, `email-digest`), canonical name first so
  consolidated brains are unaffected. Wired into both the single-slug filter and
  the multi-page `listPages` loop. Unknown types pass through unchanged.

- **`normalize-block.ts`** — a pure, exported pre-pass that collapses the
  header + indented-body block into the canonical `**Name** (HH:MM): <body>`
  line shape the existing `bold-paren-time` pattern matches; the per-message
  date fills in downstream via `fallbackDate`. Wired as the first step in
  `parseConversation`, guarded by a strict signature check (the paren-group must
  END the line) so it is a **no-op on already-canonical content** — verified by
  test. 12h am/pm is normalized to 24h; the day-of-week is dropped.

## Why a normalizer, not a 15th pattern

The 14 built-ins are line-oriented (one matched line = one message with inline
body). A header-then-body-block is a different structure that doesn't fit the
line-by-line registry. Normalizing once, up front, keeps every downstream
pattern, the scorer, and the segmenter unchanged.

## Result (verified on a ~13.7K-page comms brain whose facts table was empty)

- Routing: `--types slack` went from matching **0 pages → 20 pages** considered.
- Parsing: a 12-page Slack sample went from **0/12 pages parsed (`no_match`)**
  to **12/12 (`regex_match`), 103 messages, 13 segments**.
- Extraction: **58 facts** across the 5 segment-bearing pages, **~$0.09**
  (~$0.0076 per page considered). The facts table went from **0 → 58 facts
  across 16 entities**.
- `find_trajectory(<entity>)` returns a populated points list for a
  brain-owner / local caller where it previously returned empty.

Scope note (out of this PR): the extractor emits mostly free-text facts with
`valid_from` = extraction date, so `find_trajectory`'s typed-metric trend +
drift features stay sparse until claim-date + typed-claim extraction land; and
private-visibility facts are world-filtered for remote callers by design. This
PR's job is upstream of all that — it makes the pipeline produce facts at all
on collector-native page shapes.

## Tests

- `normalize-block`: 13 unit tests (detection, multi-paragraph collapse,
  title-drop, 12h→24h, day-of-week drop, no-op on canonical, empty body) +
  2 `parseConversation` integration tests (block parses; canonical unchanged).
- `pageTypesForAllowed`: 7 unit tests (expansion, canonical-first, dedup,
  passthrough, empty).
- `bun run typecheck` clean; `bun run verify` 30/30; touched suites green.

---
🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: feat(conversation-facts): parse Slack block format + route granular collector page-types

body:
## Keyless (Entra/AAD) auth for the azure-openai embedding recipe

Subscriptions that enforce `disableLocalAuth=true` via Azure Policy reject api-key auth entirely, so the existing `azure-openai` recipe (api-key only) is unusable there. This adds an Entra/AAD path with no new dependency.

### What changed
- **`recipes/azure-openai.ts`** — when `AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY` is absent (or `AZURE_OPENAI_USE_ENTRA=1`), mint a short-lived AAD bearer token via `az account get-access-token --resource https://cognitiveservices.azure.com`, cached ~45 min. `resolveAuth` is synchronous, so `execSync` is the seam. It returns an `Authorization: Bearer …` pair, which the gateway already maps to the SDK's native bearer path (it strips the prefix and re-adds it — no double-auth). `AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY` moves required → optional.
- **`config.ts` + `build-gateway-config.ts`** — add `azure_openai_endpoint` / `azure_openai_deployment` / `azure_openai_use_entra` config keys, folded into the gateway env (same pattern as `openai_api_key`) so the recipe works in any shell (incl. non-interactive agent shells) without per-shell env. Non-secret only; the token is minted at request time.

### Caller requirements
`az login` + the **Cognitive Services OpenAI User** role on the resource.

### Verification
End-to-end against a real keyless Azure OpenAI `text-embedding-3-large` deployment (`disableLocalAuth=true`): `gbrain init --pglite --embedding-model azure-openai:text-embedding-3-large`, then `import` + `query` retrieved the page (hybrid score ~0.80). Persistence verified with all `AZURE_OPENAI_*` env vars unset (config supplies them; `az` supplies the token).
title: feat(azure): keyless (Entra/AAD) auth for the azure-openai embedding recipe

body:
The `schema-evolution` and `schema-author` skill docs only list `gbrain-base` and `gbrain-recommended` as the read-only bundled packs to fork before mutating. gbrain now ships **7** bundled read-only packs, so the "fork first" guidance + matching anti-patterns are stale — an agent reading them could try to mutate one of the other 5 (`gbrain-base-v2`, `gbrain-creator`, `gbrain-investor`, `gbrain-engineer`, `gbrain-everything`) and hit a confusing `PACK_READONLY` refusal the docs never warned about.

Updates both docs to list all 7. Docs-only; no behavior change.

body:
## Summary

The two timeline parsers — `extractTimelineFromContent` (fs walk, `src/commands/extract.ts`) and `parseTimelineEntries` (DB / `put_page` auto-timeline, `src/core/link-extraction.ts`) — only recognized two shapes:

- bold-pipe bullets: `- **YYYY-MM-DD** | Source — Summary`
- titled headers: `### YYYY-MM-DD — Title`

Brains that keep people interaction logs as plain `- YYYY-MM-DD: summary` bullets and session logs as date-only `### YYYY-MM-DD` headers (entry body in the prose below) extracted **zero** timeline entries. The `timeline_entries` graph stayed empty and `gbrain doctor` reported timeline coverage near 0% despite rich, well-dated content on every entity page.

This PR teaches **both** parsers two additional formats, kept in lockstep so the fs and DB paths agree:

- **Format 3 — plain interaction-log bullet** `- YYYY-MM-DD: summary`
  - splits a trailing markdown-link source off the summary (`… — [slack](url)`)
  - date ranges `A to B` → start date
  - month-only `YYYY-MM` → `YYYY-MM-01`
- **Format 4 — date-only / range session-log header** `### YYYY-MM-DD` (and `### A to B`)
  - first non-empty prose line becomes the summary, the block becomes the detail
  - mutually exclusive with the existing titled-header format (no double counting)

All dates are normalized and calendar-validated before insertion, so the `timeline_entries.date` SQL DATE column never sees a range or partial token.

### Impact (real vault, ~410 pages, 83 person pages)

| Metric | Before | After |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `timeline_entries` | 4 | 772 |
| entity timeline coverage (`doctor`) | 1% | 88% |
| `brain_score` timeline component | 0/15 | 5/15 |

No vault-format changes were required — the parsers now meet the content where it is.

## Test plan

- [x] `bun test test/extract.test.ts test/link-extraction.test.ts` → 159 pass / 0 fail (includes new cases for interaction bullets, trailing-source split, date ranges, month-only dates, date-only session headers, titled-header non-regression, and invalid-date rejection)
- [x] `bun run typecheck` → 0 errors
- [x] Verified existing Format 1 / Format 2 behavior is unchanged

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
title: fix(extract): recognize plain-bullet interaction logs and date-only session headers in timeline extraction

body:
## Problem

The `x-to-brain` recipe's health check probes `https://api.x.com/2/users/me`, which requires **user-context OAuth**. Under an app-only bearer token (what the rest of the recipe uses), `/users/me` returns 401 — so the health check fails on every probe even when the token is valid.

## Fix

Switch the probe to `https://api.x.com/2/users/by/username/$X_HANDLE`, which works under app-only bearer and matches how the rest of the recipe authenticates.

Requires the operator to set `$X_HANDLE` alongside `$X_BEARER_TOKEN`.
title: fix(recipes/x-to-brain): use /users/by/username for app-only bearer health check

body:
## What

`gbrain extract timeline` only recovers entries from **in-body** date lines
(`- **YYYY-MM-DD** | …`). On comms- and calendar-heavy brains the date lives in
the **frontmatter** or the **filename** (slug `2026-04-24-…`), not the prose, so
those pages produce zero page-level `timeline` entries even though the page is
firmly dated.

This adds an opt-in `--infer-dates` flag (DB-source) that anchors **one** timeline
entry at a page's already-computed `effective_date` — but only for pages whose
in-body parse returns nothing, and only when the date is trustworthy.

## How

- New pure helper `deriveTimelineAnchor()` in `link-extraction.ts`. Returns a
  single `TimelineCandidate` from `(slug, title, effectiveDate, effectiveDateSource)`,
  or `null`.
  - Fires only for trustworthy sources: frontmatter (`event_date` / `date` /
    `published`) or the `filename` date. **Never** the `updated_at` `fallback`
    (that's link-churn noise, not when the thing happened).
  - Summary = page title, falling back to the slug basename.
- `extractTimelineFromDB` applies it **only on the zero-entry path**
  (`entries.length === 0 && inferDates`), so it can never shadow a real in-body
  timeline.
- `getPage()` now projects `effective_date` / `effective_date_source` in **both**
  engines (Postgres + PGLite) so the helper has its inputs — kept in lockstep per
  the engine-parity invariant.

## Why opt-in

Default OFF for back-compat (mirrors `--include-frontmatter`). Brains that want
to populate timeline from slug/frontmatter dates opt in; nothing changes for
existing runs.

## Scope — what this does and does NOT touch

This populates the page-level **`timeline`** table, which feeds `get_timeline`
and the brain-score `timeline_coverage` component. Verified on a comms-heavy
~13.7K-page brain: timeline yield goes from **1 → 11,005** entries and the
brain-score timeline component rises **0/15 → 12/15** (overall 75 → 87).

It deliberately does **not** touch:
- **`find_trajectory`** — that reads the `facts` table keyed by `entity_slug`
  (`claim_metric` / `event_type`), a separate pipeline (`extract_facts` /
  `extract_atoms`). Page-level timeline anchors don't feed it.
- The **entity-page** `timeline_coverage` doctor check (the per-person/company
  chronology) — populated by `--from-meetings` / fact extraction, not by this.

## Tests

- 6 new unit tests for `deriveTimelineAnchor` (trustworthy sources, fallback
  rejection, null/unparseable dates, title→slug-basename fallback).
- `bun run typecheck` clean; `bun run verify` 30/30 green; `link-extraction.test.ts`
  green (133 tests).

## Follow-ups (not in this PR)

- The `--stale` DB path (`extractStaleFromDB`) uses a projected row that may not
  carry `effective_date`; wiring `--infer-dates` there is a natural follow-up.
- Summary text for inferred entries is the page title; a future pass could
  synthesize a richer one-liner.

---
🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: feat(extract): --infer-dates anchors timeline from a page's content date when its body has none

body:
## What

`gbrain frontmatter validate <single-file>` derives the expected slug from the **absolute** filesystem path, producing bogus `root/<abs-path>` slugs and a false `SLUG_MISMATCH`. The pre-commit hook installed by `gbrain frontmatter install-hook` validates staged files one at a time, so this rejects **every commit** in a markdown brain repo (only bypassable with `--no-verify`).

Reopens #565 (closed, but still reproducible on the single-file `validate` path). Present since v0.32.0; reproduced on **v0.42.51**.

## Repro

From a brain root, with a file whose slug is correct:

```
$ gbrain frontmatter validate companies/readme.md
[SLUG_MISMATCH] Frontmatter slug "companies/readme" does not match path-derived slug "root/brain/companies/readme"
```

The file's `slug: companies/readme` is correct; the path-derived slug is the absolute path minus the leading `/`.

## Cause

`src/commands/frontmatter.ts`:

```js
const expectedSlug = slugifyPath(relative(resolve(target), file) || file);
```

`collectFiles` returns `[target]` for a single file, so `file === resolve(target)` → `relative(...)` is `""` → falls back to `|| file` (the absolute path) → `slugifyPath` strips the leading `/` → `root/...`. Validating a subdirectory was also wrong (slugs relative to the subdir, not the brain root).

## Fix

Walk up from the target to the brain root (nearest `.git`) and derive the slug with `relative(brainRoot, file)`, falling back to `basename(file)`. This matches the brain-root resolution `runAudit`/`runGenerate` already use and how sync/extract compute slugs. Files above the brain root fall back to `basename` instead of emitting a `../`-prefixed slug.

(Note: `runAudit`/`runGenerate` have a near-identical walk-up inline — could be unified into the new `findBrainRoot` helper in a follow-up. Kept this PR scoped to the fix.)

## Tests

`test/frontmatter-validate-slug-565.test.ts`: single-file nested slug, directory validation, and the no-`.git` fallback. All 3 pass; the single-file case fails on the default branch without the fix.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: fix(frontmatter): derive validate slug from brain root, not absolute path (#565)

body:
## Problem

The provider-agnostic tool loop (`gateway.chat` → `generateText`, used for **all non-Anthropic models** — including local vLLM models in `dream` synthesis) crashes with AI SDK v6 `AI_MissingToolResultsError`:

> `[chat(openrouter:…)] Tool results are missing for tool calls: chatcmpl-tool-…`

`generateText` validates that every assistant `tool-call` is resolved by a `tool-result` in the **immediately-following** message. When that invariant is violated the whole subagent job dies — **and every retry too**, because the persisted history is replayed verbatim and re-throws the identical error (the classic `retrying… permanently failed` cascade). On a local-model dream setup this killed the large majority of synthesis subagents (~89% dead over 10 days in my brain).

Three real triggers produce an unbalanced history:

1. **Crash / force-evict mid-turn** — the assistant turn (N tool-calls) is persisted but its tool-result message never is, so replay loads an assistant turn with no following results.
2. **Truncation** (`finishReason: 'length'`) emitting a partial parallel tool-call batch.
3. **Provider-duplicated / dropped tool-call IDs** — observed with vLLM parallel tool-use on local models (e.g. Qwen-NVFP4).

## Fix

Add `repairToolPairing()`, a normalization pass applied right before `toModelMessages()` in `chat()`. For every assistant tool-call with no matching result in the next message, it injects a synthetic `error-text` tool-result so the model recovers gracefully on the next turn instead of the job dying. It is a **no-op on already-balanced histories** (the overwhelmingly common case).

This mirrors the AI SDK's own guidance for `AI_MissingToolResultsError` (every tool-call must be resolved before the next model call) and matches the robustness the native-Anthropic path already had.

## Tests

`test/ai/gateway-toolcall-pairing.test.ts` — drives the **real** AI SDK v6 `generateText` via `MockLanguageModelV3` (no network/keys), same pattern as the existing `gateway-tools-schema.test.ts`:

- no-op on a balanced history
- synthesizes results when the assistant turn has no following tool message (crash/evict)
- back-fills **only the missing IDs** of a partial parallel batch (truncation / dropped IDs)
- a previously-fatal unbalanced history now passes `generateText`

All 4 new tests pass; the 32 existing `test/ai/gateway-*` tests remain green.

## Possible follow-up

Reducing parallel tool-call fan-out for local models (the trigger amplifier) would further cut churn, but the invariant repair is the correctness fix and stands alone.
title: fix(gateway): recover unbalanced tool-call histories instead of hard-failing the job

body:
## Summary

`gbrain sync` over a quiet vault (no new commits since the last sync) successfully runs, but `sources.last_sync_at` is **not** written. This makes `gbrain doctor sync_freshness` (warn 24h / fail 72h) flag the source as stale for as long as the vault is quiet — even though the sync IS working.

The D4 invariant ("never advance `last_commit` on partial import", documented in `sync.ts:2673-2680`) is preserved. `last_sync_at` is a separate monitoring signal (read by `doctor` only), distinct from the import-converged bookmark.

## Reproduction (5 lines, no gbrain install required)

```bash
# 1. Setup a fresh obsidian source + commit a single .md file
# 2. gbrain sync --source obsidian   # first_sync, last_sync_at = NOW
# 3. (do nothing) gbrain sync --source obsidian   # up_to_date
# 4. SELECT last_sync_at FROM sources WHERE id = 'obsidian';
# 5. Observed: still pinned to step 2.  Expected: bumped to step 3.
```

Verified live on Bruce's brain (commit `9bf96db8` = `v0.42.51.0`, 2026-06-21 17:09 CST):
- `last_sync_at` stuck at `2026-06-20 07:14:28 UTC` for 25.7h
- `gbrain doctor` → `[WARN] sync_freshness: ... 25h ago`
- `gbrain sync --repo` runs every 15 min via WSL crontab (`*/15 * * * *`), reports "Already up to date", but never writes `last_sync_at`

## Fix

`src/commands/sync.ts` line ~1786 (the `up_to_date` early-return path):

```typescript
// Before:
if (lastCommit === headCommit && !versionMismatch && !versionNeverSet && !hasDetachedWorkingTreeChanges) {
  return { status: 'up_to_date', ... };  // no DB write
}

// After:
if (lastCommit === headCommit && !versionMismatch && !versionNeverSet && !hasDetachedWorkingTreeChanges) {
  // v0.42.52.0: bump last_sync_at as a heartbeat on every successful 0-changes sync.
  // D4 invariant preserved: only last_sync_at is touched, not last_commit.
  if (opts.sourceId) {
    await engine.executeRaw(
      `UPDATE sources SET last_sync_at = now() WHERE id = $1`,
      [opts.sourceId],
    );
  }
  return { status: 'up_to_date', ... };
}
```

Diff: 2 files, +97/-0 lines (additive only).

## Test

`test/sync.test.ts` adds 1 describe block with 1 test:
- Sets up a PGLite engine + a quiet vault
- Runs `performSync` twice with 1.1s gap
- Asserts `status: 'first_sync'` then `status: 'up_to_date'`
- Asserts `last_sync_at` advanced between runs
- Asserts `last_commit` did **not** change (D4 invariant preserved)

Pattern matches the existing #1970 test scaffold (`PGLiteEngine + resetPgliteState + mkRepo`).

## Verification (local)

```
$ bun test test/sync.test.ts -t 'v0.42.52.0'
(pass) v0.42.52.0: 0-changes sync bumps last_sync_at heartbeat (D4 invariant preserved)
 1 pass / 0 fail / 7 expect() calls

$ bun test test/sync.test.ts
 67 pass / 0 fail / 188 expect() calls
```

No regression on existing tests (#1970: 7 pass, all sync tests: 67 pass).

## Workaround (already deployed)

Until this PR merges, Bruce runs this in production:

```cron
# WSL crontab
17 * * * * /usr/bin/docker exec gbrain-pg psql -U gbrain -d gbrain -c \
  "UPDATE sources SET last_sync_at = NOW() WHERE local_path IS NOT NULL AND last_sync_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '6 hours';" \
  >> /home/lost9999/.gbrain/run/sync-heartbeat.log 2>&1
```

## Related context

- v0.42.51.0 (#2255) added the "in-progress sync via live lock" signal to `sync_freshness`. **This PR complements that**: a sync that's running reports ok via the lock; a sync that ran but did nothing also reports ok via the heartbeat.
- v0.42.36.0 (#1794) introduced the D4 invariant. The intent (no partial advance) is preserved.

## Checklist

- [x] Reproduction documented
- [x] Fix is minimal (12 lines in sync.ts, additive)
- [x] D4 invariant explicitly preserved in test
- [x] No regression on existing tests (67 pass)
- [x] Workaround documented
- [x] Follows upstream commit message convention
title: v0.42.52.0 fix(sync): bump last_sync_at heartbeat on 0-changes sync
