body:
## Problem

A production worker logged **400+ "crashes" / 24h** — one every ~5 minutes — all with the same chain:

```
[cycle.lint] done
Promotion error: No database connection: connect() has not been called.
Job NNNN (autopilot-cycle) released after infrastructure abort (lock-renewal-failed); stall detector will requeue (no attempt burned)
write CONNECTION_ENDED aws-1-...pooler.supabase.com:6543
worker_exited code=1 ... likely_cause=runtime_error
```

These aren't data-losing (jobs requeue, no attempt burned — `withRefreshingLock`'s designed safety release) but they are noisy and throughput-killing: the worker restarts every cycle and the autopilot queue backs up.

## Root cause

`withRefreshingLock`'s refresh tick fires every `(TTL/6)` ms — **~5 min** for the default 30-min cycle lock. But the read pool's `idle_timeout` is **20s** (`connection-manager.ts`), so the connection is *always* reaped between ticks. Against a transaction-mode pooler (PgBouncer / Supavisor :6543) the reconnect races and postgres.js raises **`write CONNECTION_ENDED`**.

That throw escapes both `engineSelectOne` (the SELECT-1 heartbeat) and `handle.refresh()`, trips `withRefreshingLock`'s catch, sets `healthOk=false`, and the worker voluntarily releases + exits code 1.

The existing retry matcher (`retry-matcher.ts`) caught the **downstream** `No database connection` GBrainError — but only *after* the pool was already nulled. The **root** `CONNECTION_ENDED` had no matching pattern, so the first (recoverable) error escaped retry entirely.

## Fix (two minimal, idiomatic changes)

1. **`retry-matcher.ts`** — add `CONNECTION_ENDED` (postgres.js error *code* + message pattern). Benefits every `withRetry` call site, not just locks.

2. **`db-lock.ts`** — wrap `refresh()` and the SELECT-1 heartbeat in `withRetry` with a duck-typed `reconnect()` callback (PostgresEngine-private; `undefined` for PGLite, which can't lose a socket). A reaped connection self-heals (rebuild pool, retry) instead of killing the lock holder. Mirrors the engine-level `batchRetry` idiom from #1570.

## Tests

- `+2` CONNECTION_ENDED cases in `retry-matcher.test.ts` (code form + message form).
- `tsc --noEmit` clean.
- `retry-matcher` (17 pass) + `db-lock-refresh` + `worker-lock-renewal` + `worker-lock-renewal-shape` suites green.

## Notes / alternatives considered

- **Bumping `idle_timeout`**: rejected — fights the pooler's own reaping and just moves the race.
- **Pinning refresh to `withReservedConnection`** (the `db-lock.ts:552` "Lane B follow-up" TODO): heavier surface; `withRetry`+reconnect achieves the same self-heal with far less risk and reuses the existing primitive. The reserved-connection pin is still a reasonable future hardening.
title: fix(db-lock): self-heal cycle-lock refresh on pooler-reaped CONNECTION_ENDED

body:
## Problem

`gbrain think` prints `## Gaps` twice. The synthesis prompt asks the model for a Gaps section *inside* the answer body **and** for a separate structured `gaps` array, then both render paths print both — the CLI human output (`src/commands/think.ts`) and the `--save` page (`persistSynthesis` in `src/core/think/index.ts`). You get a prose Gaps block immediately followed by a second bullet-list Gaps block on every `think` call.

## Fix

Make the structured `gaps` array the single source of truth.

- **Prompt** (`src/core/think/prompt.ts`): gaps are routed into the structured `gaps` array, not an answer-body section. The answer schema drops `Gaps` from its section list, and the `gaps` field is nudged toward specific, cited statements so nothing is lost by removing the prose duplicate. The `willSave` and calibration lines are updated to match (calibration text still contains "Calibration", so `think-with-calibration.test.ts` stays green).
- **Defensive strip** (`src/core/think/index.ts`): new exported `stripGapsSection(answer)` removes any `## Gaps` section a model still emits — any heading level, case-insensitive, bounded by the next same/higher heading, no-op when absent. Both render sites (the CLI and `persistSynthesis`) call it, so the dedup is structural and doesn't depend on the model obeying the prompt.

## Tests

`test/think-gaps.test.ts` (hermetic, no DB/API): the strip helper across heading levels / case / no-section / mid-document / a `## Gaps in coverage` false-match guard, the one-render-only repro, and the prompt contract (schema no longer lists a body Gaps section; structured `gaps` still required; Conflicts + Hard rules preserved).

## What it doesn't change

Retrieval is untouched (no search / ranking / embedding changes), so the retrieval eval-replay gate doesn't apply. `--json` output is unchanged (it already returns `answer` + `gaps` separately). `bun run verify` (29/29) and the full `test/think-*` suite (119 pass) are green.
title: fix(think): render the Gaps section once instead of twice

body:
## Problem

`gbrain autopilot --install` generates `~/.gbrain/autopilot-run.sh`, which sources `~/.zshenv` + `~/.zshrc`/`~/.bashrc` to inherit PATH (and secrets). But interactive shell rc files commonly early-return for non-interactive shells, or guard the PATH line behind interactivity - so the gbrain CLI's own directory (`~/.bun/bin` for a bun install, an nvm bin, a custom prefix, etc.) never reaches PATH when the wrapper runs under launchd / systemd / cron.

Result: autopilot starts (the wrapper `exec`s the absolute CLI path it baked in), but its internal subprocess spawns (`gbrain <subcommand>`) can't resolve the CLI:

```
Could not resolve the gbrain CLI path. Install gbrain so it is on $PATH (e.g. /usr/local/bin/gbrain) ...
```

The daemon shows `active`, but every cycle's subprocess work silently no-ops - continuous capture quietly does nothing. (Observed on a Linux systemd user-service install with a bun-installed CLI whose `~/.bashrc` early-returns for non-interactive shells.)

## Fix

`writeWrapperScript()` already resolves the absolute CLI path for `exec`. Reuse it: export `dirname(resolvedCliPath)` onto PATH, placed AFTER the rc sourcing (so rc can't clobber it) and BEFORE `exec`. Derived from the path the installer already found, so it works for any install location (bun, npm-global, nvm, custom prefix):

```sh
source ~/.zshrc 2>/dev/null || source ~/.bashrc 2>/dev/null || true
export PATH='<cli-dir>':"$PATH"
exec '<cli-path>' autopilot --repo '<repo>'
```

## Tests

- `test/autopilot-install.test.ts`: source-level guardrail (matching the existing `#966` env-source-order wrapper test) - asserts the wrapper exports the CLI dir on PATH, after rc sourcing and before `exec`.
- `bun run typecheck`: clean.
- Existing autopilot-install suite: green (6 tests).
title: fix(autopilot): put the gbrain CLI dir on PATH in the generated wrapper

body:
## Problem

`PGLiteEngine.putPage()` calls `rowToPage(rows[0])` unconditionally after an `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE ... RETURNING`. PGLite can return **zero** rows from that statement in no-op / trigger edge cases. When that happens `rows[0]` is `undefined`, and `rowToPage` throws:

```
undefined is not an object (evaluating 'row.deleted_at')
```

Because this fires inside the per-file import path, the error aborts the upsert and the file is **silently skipped** during sync — it never gets embedded.

On a real ~5,100-file monorepo index this was failing ~19% of files (985/5148). Every affected file reported 100%-looking progress elsewhere while quietly dropping out of the index.

## Fix

`getPage()` already guards the empty-rows case. `putPage()` was missing the parallel guard. The upsert **did** write the row, so on an empty `RETURNING` we re-read it via `getPage()` instead of crashing, and only throw if the re-read also comes back empty (a genuine invariant violation).

```ts
if (rows.length === 0) {
  const reread = await this.getPage(slug, { sourceId });
  if (reread) return reread;
  throw new Error(`putPage: RETURNING produced no row for ${sourceId}/${slug}`);
}
```

## Verification

- `tsc --noEmit` clean on `pglite-engine.ts`.
- Applied locally against a 5,148-file index: the previously-failing 985 files recovered to 0 import errors, 100% embedded.

Minimal, single-function change; no behavior change on the happy path (non-empty `RETURNING`).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: fix(pglite): guard putPage against zero-row RETURNING

body:
## Problem

`searchKeyword()` ranks only `content_chunks.search_vector` (chunk body text). Page titles are indexed at **weight A** in `pages.search_vector`, but that vector is never queried by the keyword path, and title text is never copied into any chunk at import.

Result: an exact-title query returns nothing from `gbrain search`, while hybrid `gbrain query` ranks the same page highly (it uses embedding similarity, not the keyword index).

Repro (real page in my brain titled around "agentic AI economy"):

```
$ gbrain search "agentic AI economy"
No results.

$ gbrain query "what do we have on the agentic economy?"
[0.91] inbox/2026-05-30-0b5d6ecf
```

The title tokens exist in `pages.search_vector` (weight A) but are unreachable through the keyword path.

## Fix

Add a page-grain `title_hits` CTE to `searchKeyword()` that unions in pages whose `pages.search_vector` matches but that have **no** matching text chunk (`NOT EXISTS` anti-join):

- Pages that already match on a body chunk rank **exactly as before** — zero regression. The arm only *adds* title-only pages the chunk-only query missed entirely.
- Score scales by `ts_rank(pages.search_vector, query) * sourceFactor`, consistent with the chunk arm, so a weak title hit doesn't automatically dominate a strong body hit.
- Snippet reports `chunk_source = 'compiled_truth'` (the snippet's true origin) to avoid introducing a new enum value downstream.
- Synthetic `chunk_id = -1`, `chunk_index = 0` for title-only rows (never collide with real chunk ids).
- Symbol/language filters are chunk-grain and intentionally omitted from this arm.

## Verification

- Failing case now returns the page at rank 1.0 (was `No results`).
- Body-word search unchanged: `gbrain search "agentic"` still ranks via the chunk vector at ~0.38 (proves the title arm is correctly anti-joined and not leaking into pages that already have body hits).
- `bunx tsc --noEmit`: clean.
- `bun test` on the search suites: 49 tests pass (`commands-search.test.ts`, `search-lang-symbol-kind.test.ts`, `utils.test.ts`).
- Independently reproduced and validated by a second agent against the same live DB.

## Notes for reviewers

The anti-join keeps the change narrow and reversible — it touches only the previously-empty result set. Happy to adjust the snippet source label, the `ts_rank` weighting, or fold title tokens into chunk vectors at import instead if you'd prefer that direction.
title: fix(search): keyword search ignores pages.search_vector, exact-title queries return No results

body:
## Summary

Adds support for Chinese, Japanese, and Korean (CJK) entity names in the mention extraction pipeline. Currently, CJK names are invisible to the gazetteer because:

1. **TOKEN_RE** (`/[a-zA-Z0-9]+/g`) cannot tokenize CJK characters
2. **MIN_NAME_LENGTH = 4** filters out all CJK names (typically 2-3 chars)
3. **tokenizeTitle()** returns `[]` for pure CJK titles, excluding them from the gazetteer

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/core/by-mention.ts` | Add `hasCJK()` / `cjkCharCount()` helpers; lower min length to 2 for CJK; fix `tokenizeTitle()` to treat pure CJK titles as single tokens; add CJK substring matching pass in `findMentionedEntities` |
| `src/core/extract-ner.ts` | NER extraction falls back to plain `mentions` links when no schema pack is available (previously returned 0 links) |

## Verification

```
$ gbrain extract links --by-mention --source db
Mentions: created 27 links from 456 pages against gazetteer of 3 first-token buckets

$ gbrain list --type person
people/shuang-xuetao    person  双雪涛
people/naval            person  纳瓦尔
```

## Backward Compatibility

- English entity extraction is completely unchanged
- The CJK scan pass only activates when CJK-titled entities exist in the gazetteer
- NER fallback to `mentions` type only triggers when no schema pack with inference regex is available
title: feat: CJK entity extraction for Chinese/Japanese/Korean names

body:
## Problem

`gbrain code-def <symbol>` returns **0 results for methods, constructors, fields, C structs, and Swift protocols** — i.e. most of the symbols in an object-oriented codebase. `code-def <class>` and `code-def <function>` work fine. So on a Java / C++ / Swift project, `code-def` silently can't find a method even though it's fully indexed and `code-refs` finds it.

## Root cause

`content_chunks.symbol_type` is produced by `normalizeSymbolType()` in `src/core/chunkers/code.ts`, which canonicalizes only a subset of tree-sitter node types and lets the rest fall through:

```js
return type.replace(/_/g, ' ');
```

So node types land as space-normalized strings: `method_declaration` → `'method declaration'`, `method_definition` → `'method definition'`, `constructor_declaration` → `'constructor declaration'`, `field_definition` → `'field definition'`, `struct_specifier` → `'struct specifier'`, `protocol_declaration` → `'protocol declaration'`.

`code-def`'s `DEF_TYPES` filter in `src/commands/code-def.ts` only lists the canonical names, so `WHERE cc.symbol_type IN (...)` excludes every one of those. (The plain `'struct'` entry already in `DEF_TYPES` never matched anything either — C structs are stored as `'struct specifier'`.)

## Fix

Add the fallthrough definition forms to `DEF_TYPES`. Read-path only — no schema change, no reindex; it surfaces symbols already present in `content_chunks`.

## Verification

On a brain with an indexed Java codebase, `code-def <methodName>` went 0 → N, returning real `[java] path/File.java:line (method declaration)` definition sites. Class/function lookups unchanged. `bun test test/code-def-refs.test.ts test/code-edges.test.ts` → 18 pass / 0 fail; `tsc --noEmit` clean.

Follow-up (not in this PR): qualified queries like `code-def Class.method` still miss because `symbol_name` stores the short name; matching the trailing dotted segment would close that.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: fix(code-def): include method/constructor/field/struct/protocol definitions

body:
## Summary

- **Add chat + expansion touchpoints to the Zhipu recipe** — the recipe only declared embedding touchpoints, so `isAvailable('chat')` and `isAvailable('expansion')` returned false even with `ZHIPUAI_API_KEY` set. Adds GLM-4.7, GLM-4.7-flash, GLM-5.1, GLM-5-turbo as chat/expansion models.

- **Fix silent expansion failure for all openai-compatible providers** — `generateObject()` uses `response_format: json_schema` which most openai-compatible endpoints don't support. The call fails silently and `expand()` returns only the original query. For openai-compat recipes, fall back to `generateText()` + manual JSON parse. Native providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google) keep the existing `generateObject()` path unchanged.

This affects every openai-compatible provider with expansion enabled: Zhipu/GLM, DeepSeek, Groq, Together, Ollama, and any future recipe using the `openai-compatible` implementation.

## Test plan

- [x] Confirmed `gbrain providers list` shows zhipu with EMBED/EXPAND/CHAT all `yes` + `ready`
- [x] Confirmed `expand()` returns 3-4 expanded queries via GLM-4.7 through Z.ai endpoint
- [x] Confirmed `isAvailable('expansion')` and `isAvailable('chat')` return true with `ZHIPUAI_API_KEY` set
- [x] Confirmed native providers (Anthropic/OpenAI) still use the `generateObject()` path (no behavioral change)
- [ ] Verify other openai-compat providers (DeepSeek, Groq) also benefit from the generateText fallback

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: feat(zhipu): add chat/expansion support + fix expand() for openai-compat providers

body:
## Summary

Web-dashboard counterpart to the v0.41.28.0 federated-read CLI (PR #1592). Brings source visibility + read-scope management into the admin SPA so operators don't need a terminal.

**Stacked on #1592.** This branch was cut from the v0.41.28.0 branch, so until #1592 lands this PR's diff also shows the 2 v0.41.28.0 commits (`feat(auth): federated_read CLI commands` + its version bump). The net-new content here is the 4 admin commits. Once #1592 merges to master I'll rebase and only the admin commits remain. Review can focus on:

- `feat(admin): Sources tab + Federation management UI`
- `fix(admin): Sources tab + federation picker work for push-only sources`
- `fix(admin): align checkbox rows in Manage federated reads modal`
- `chore: bump version and changelog (v0.41.29.0)`

## What it adds

**Sources tab** (new sidebar entry): read-only table of every source — pages, chunks, embedding coverage, last-sync state — plus reverse-lookup of which OAuth clients write to each source and which read it via federation.

**Federation management** in the per-agent drawer: a Federation section shows a client's write source + federated_read list; a "Manage reads" button opens a source-checkbox modal. Save routes through the same race-safe `setFederatedReadCore` atomic-SQL path the CLI uses — dashboard edits and `gbrain auth set-federated-read` have identical guarantees (the v0.41.28.0 HIGH-severity race fix, soft-delete guard, and source-id shape validation all apply to the web path).

**Push-only aware**: sources that receive content over MCP (`put_page` / capture / `/ingest`) rather than `gbrain sync` have no `local_path`. The tab and the federation picker list them correctly, and the "Last Sync" cell shows a neutral "push-only" instead of a misleading "never synced" warning. (An earlier draft filtered `WHERE local_path IS NOT NULL`, which emptied both surfaces on push-only brains — fixed.)

## Backend (serve-http.ts)

5 new routes, all behind the existing admin-session gate:

```
GET  /admin/api/sources                          # sources + sync/embed stats (no local_path filter)
GET  /admin/api/agents/federated-read            # clients + their scopes
POST /admin/api/agents/:id/grant-read            # add one source
POST /admin/api/agents/:id/revoke-read           # remove one source
POST /admin/api/agents/:id/set-federated-read    # replace whole list
```

The mutating three import the `*Core` helpers from `auth.ts`; the sources route reuses `buildSyncStatusReport` from the sync command lib (which does no disk I/O, so null-`local_path` sources report fine).

## Frontend (admin/src/)

- `pages/Sources.tsx` (new) — sources table + writers/readers reverse-lookup + push-only label
- `pages/Agents.tsx` — Federation section + FederationModal in the agent drawer; checkbox rows pinned to a fixed gutter, long ids ellipsis-truncate
- `App.tsx` + `api.ts` — Sources nav entry + 5 API client functions
- SPA rebuilt (242KB → 73KB gzip)

## Test plan

- [x] `bun run typecheck` clean
- [x] `bun run verify` — 28/28 checks green
- [x] `bun test test/auth-federated-read.test.ts test/oauth.test.ts` — 140 pass (covers the `*Core` helpers the new routes call)
- [x] Live on a self-hosted multi-source deployment: Sources tab lists all sources with push-only labels; Manage reads modal lists all sources as checkboxes; check/uncheck + Save writes correctly and the drawer refreshes

## Note for the maintainer

From a fork (read-only access upstream). Built + verified on a live self-hosted gbrain while setting up a per-client-company source model. Includes a Caddy SSE operator note in the CHANGELOG (gzip buffering hangs the dashboard's Live Activity feed; the fix is host-side Caddy config, not repo code).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: v0.41.29.0 feat(admin): Sources tab + federation management UI (stacked on #1592)

body:
## Summary
- Raises the default content-sanity soft-block threshold from 500KB to 2MB so large-but-valid markdown pages can still be chunked and embedded by default.
- Scopes the CLI hard-exit watchdog to the `engine.disconnect()` cleanup phase instead of arming it before command execution.
- Clears stale `embed_skip` frontmatter when a page no longer exceeds the active content-sanity threshold.
- Fixes non-hermetic verification/test behavior found while validating this change on macOS and in nested temporary fixture directories.
- Adds regression coverage for threshold behavior, stale `embed_skip` recovery, CLI lifecycle timing, timeout fallback behavior, and git/workspace fixture isolation.

## Problem
GBrain currently treats pages above the default 500KB content-sanity limit as unsafe to embed. That is a useful guardrail for pathological inputs, but 500KB is low enough to soft-block legitimate long-form project pages, transcripts, imported docs, and generated knowledge pages. When this happens, the page can appear to be saved while having no searchable chunk/embedding coverage.

There are two adjacent recovery issues:

1. A page that was previously written with `embed_skip` can remain permanently skipped even if its content or the operator's configured threshold later changes enough to make it embeddable.
2. The CLI hard-exit watchdog was armed before the command handler ran, so long-running valid work could be terminated after 10 seconds while reporting a misleading disconnect-timeout message.

The test suite also exposed a separate verification reliability problem. The macOS shell-timeout fallback used by the parallel verify/test wrappers could convert successful child checks into rc=143 when cleaning up the watchdog process. Some tests also depended on ambient repository or environment state when fixtures were created inside directories that happened to sit under an unrelated ancestor git worktree.

## Approach
This PR keeps the safety model, but moves the default threshold to a more practical value and makes recovery explicit:

- `DEFAULT_BYTES_BLOCK` is now 2MB. Operators can still override it with `content_sanity.bytes_block` or `GBRAIN_PAGE_BLOCK_BYTES`.
- Oversized pages above the active threshold still soft-block and preserve the delete-chunks invariant.
- Pages below the active threshold have stale `embed_skip` removed during import so normal chunk/embed recovery can happen.
- The CLI hard-exit timer now only guards `engine.disconnect()` cleanup.
- The parallel verify/unit wrappers now preserve the child process result before cleaning up timeout watchdogs, and true timeout still maps to rc=124.
- Tests that need isolated repositories or skill directories now pass explicit fixture roots/env instead of inheriting host state.

## Verification
- [x] `bun test` — 11,930 pass / 0 fail / 18 skip
- [x] `bun run verify` — 29 pass / 0 fail
- [x] `bun run typecheck`
- [x] `git diff --check`
- [x] `scripts/check-privacy.sh`
- [x] `scripts/check-test-real-names.sh`
- [x] `scripts/check-proposal-pii.sh`

Focused regression coverage:

- [x] `bun test test/content-sanity.test.ts test/import-file-content-sanity.test.ts test/fix-wave-structural.test.ts test/cli-should-force-exit.test.ts`
- [x] `bun test test/scripts/check-test-isolation.test.ts test/check-resolvable-cli.test.ts test/dry-fix.test.ts`
- [x] `bun test test/migrations-v0_32_2.test.ts`
- [x] `bun test test/fuzz/filesystem-validators.test.ts test/lint-content-sanity.test.ts`
title: fix: let large page embeddings recover

body:
## Summary

The `code-fence-wrap` rule in `gbrain lint` reports false positives that the matching autofixer can never resolve. After upgrading my brain to v0.35.0, `gbrain dream` printed `0 fix(es) applied, N remaining` every cycle with N=5 on a brain that contains exactly zero LLM-wrapped pages. Diagnosis: a regex flag mismatch between the detector and the fixer.

## Root cause

In `src/commands/lint.ts:125` (HEAD = v0.41.26.1), the detector uses the `/m` flag:

```ts
if (content.match(/^```(?:markdown|md)\s*\n/m) && content.match(/\n```\s*$/m)) {
```

With `/m`, `^` matches start-of-*line* and `$` matches end-of-*line*. So the detector fires on any file that contains a `\`\`\`markdown` line and ends some other line with `\`\`\``, even when those are inner code blocks inside legitimate documentation.

But the matching fixer at `src/commands/lint.ts:275-277` has **no** `/m` flag:

```ts
fixed = fixed.replace(/^```(?:markdown|md)\s*\n/, '');
fixed = fixed.replace(/\n```\s*$/, '');
```

So the fixer can only strip whole-file wrappers. Detected inner-block matches are marked `fixable: true` but `fixContent` never modifies them, and `total_fixed` never increments.

On my brain (599 markdown pages), this surfaced on 5 legitimate files:

| File | Why flagged |
|---|---|
| `ops/DECISION_REGISTRY.md` | Contains 1 inner `\`\`\`markdown` block |
| `ops/EVENING_JOURNAL_TEMPLATE.md` | Contains 4 inner `\`\`\`markdown` blocks |
| `ops/THOMAS_QUEUE_RULES.md` | Contains 1 |
| `skills/anomaly-investigation/SKILL.md` | Contains 1 |
| `projects/.../29-pricing-evidence-patch-plan.md` | Contains 1 |

None are actually LLM-wrapped. They're docs that show markdown examples in code blocks.

## Fix

Drop the `/m` flag from the detector so it matches the fixer's semantics. Whole-file wrapper detection is preserved (the existing `detects wrapping code fences` test still passes); inner code blocks no longer trigger the rule.

```diff
-  if (content.match(/^```(?:markdown|md)\s*\n/m) && content.match(/\n```\s*$/m)) {
+  if (content.match(/^```(?:markdown|md)\s*\n/) && content.match(/\n```\s*$/)) {
```

Plus a 4-line comment explaining why the regex stays without `/m`, so a future contributor doesn't "fix" it back.

## Tests

Existing `detects wrapping code fences` test continues to pass (true-positive case is unchanged).

Two new regression tests in `test/lint.test.ts`:
- `no false positive: page CONTAINS an inner \`\`\`markdown code block`
- `no false positive: multiple inner \`\`\`markdown blocks`

Both assert `code-fence-wrap` issues are not reported on docs pages with inner code blocks.

## Verification

```
$ bun test test/lint.test.ts
 20 pass
  0 fail
 25 expect() calls

$ bun run typecheck
$ tsc --noEmit
```

Before the fix, running `gbrain lint` on my brain reported 813 issues including the 5 phantom `code-fence-wrap`. After the fix (binary rebuilt locally for verification): 808 issues, zero `code-fence-wrap`. The remaining 808 are real-and-unfixable-by-design (missing frontmatter / titles / types / placeholder dates).

## Test plan

- [x] Existing `detects wrapping code fences` (true positive) still passes
- [x] New: single inner block does not flag
- [x] New: multiple inner blocks do not flag
- [x] `bun run typecheck` clean
- [x] Manual verification on a 599-page real-world brain

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: fix(lint): code-fence-wrap detector and fixer regex now agree

body:
## Summary

Four new CLI subcommands close the visibility + manageability gap on
OAuth client `federated_read` scope. Before this PR, the only way to
inspect or change "who can read which source" on a multi-source brain
was raw SQL against `oauth_clients.federated_read`.

**New surface:**

```bash
gbrain auth list-clients [--json] [--include-deleted]
gbrain auth grant-read <client> <source> [--dry-run]
gbrain auth revoke-read <client> <source> [--dry-run]
gbrain auth set-federated-read <client> "s1,s2" [--dry-run]
```

All four resolve by friendly name (the one you passed to `register-client`)
OR full `client_id`, default-hide soft-deleted clients, and validate
source ids at the boundary (so a typo can't silently land a malformed
entry in the array).

**Commits:**
1. `feat(auth): federated_read CLI commands + atomic SQL race-safety` — substantive change (CLI surface + helpers + tests).
2. `chore: bump version and changelog (v0.41.28.0)` — release metadata.

## Pre-Landing Review

Codex pre-merge review caught 1 HIGH + 4 MEDIUM findings on the first draft; all in-scope ones fixed in this PR before push.

1. **HIGH — concurrent grant/revoke race could silently restore revoked access.** Read-modify-write let racing `revoke-read X` + `grant-read Y` clobber the revoke. Fixed via atomic SQL (`array_append` / `array_remove` + NOT/ANY guard + `deleted_at IS NULL` guard + `RETURNING`). Postgres serializes both UPDATEs at the row lock.
2. **MEDIUM — soft-deleted clients still mutable.** `resolveClient` + `listClients` default-filter `deleted_at IS NULL`; mutating commands have no escape hatch.
3. **MEDIUM — source-id shape not validated.** `assertSourceExists` now calls `assertValidSourceId` before the DB existence query.
4. **MEDIUM — `register-client` seeds malformed entries.** `parseRegisterClientArgs` now validates `--source` + `--federated-read` element shape at the CLI boundary.
5. **LOW — terminal control-char injection.** New `sanitizeForTerminal` escapes ALL C0/C1/DEL bytes (including `\n` and `\t`) in human output. Applied to `printOutcome`, `listClients`, and `revokeClient` success message.

**Deferred (filed in `TODOS.md` as v0.41.28.0 follow-ups):**
- `TODO-FR-A (P2)`: no FK on `oauth_clients.federated_read` array — needs schema migration (join table).
- `TODO-FR-B (P3)`: admin `POST /admin/api/update-client-ttl` skips `deleted_at` filter — small follow-up patch.

## Test Coverage

72 cases across two test files (67 new + 3 extending an existing file):

- pure helpers: `parseSourceCsv`, `extractDryRun`, `sanitizeForTerminal`
- DB-coupled: `resolveClient` + soft-delete filter, `assertSourceExists` + shape validation
- Core fns: `grantReadCore`, `revokeReadCore`, `setFederatedReadCore` — idempotency, race-safety (the `grant+revoke serialize at row-lock` regression test), dry-run semantics, error paths
- `parseRegisterClientArgs` — `--source` + `--federated-read` shape validation + canonical case regression

All tests use the canonical PGLite block from CLAUDE.md test-isolation rules.

## Scope Drift

Scope Check: **CLEAN.** Stated intent: "add CLI for federation management". Delivered exactly that + in-scope hardening from the adversarial review. No expansion into unrelated areas.

## Documentation

No external docs reference `federated_read` management (the commands didn't exist before). Help text in `gbrain auth` lists all four new subcommands. CHANGELOG entry carries the user-facing story (ELI10 lead + numbers table + caveats + "what we caught" section per CLAUDE.md voice rules).

## Test plan

- [x] All 72 unit tests pass (`bun test test/auth-federated-read.test.ts test/auth-register-client-args.test.ts`)
- [x] 98 adjacent oauth tests still pass (`bun test test/oauth.test.ts test/oauth-confidential-client.test.ts`)
- [x] Typecheck clean (`bun run typecheck`)
- [x] Full verify gate passes (`bun run verify` — 28/28 checks green)
- [x] Live-fire against a multi-user gbrain deployment:
  - `gbrain auth list-clients` — prints registered clients with scope details
  - `gbrain auth grant-read <client> <source>` → "Did grant"; verified in DB
  - Re-running the same grant → "No change: already reads"
  - `--dry-run` variant → "[dry-run] Would grant"; DB unchanged
  - Malformed source id → exit 1, "Invalid source_id" with paste-ready hint

## Note for the maintainer

Submitting from a fork since I only have read access on the upstream repo. Branch was developed while resolving a real "I need to add a new source and share it with multiple teammates" question on a self-hosted gbrain; the CLI commands were exactly the missing surface that turned a multi-line SQL task into a one-second operator command. Built + tested + reviewed in one session.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: v0.41.28.0 feat(auth): grant-read / revoke-read / set-federated-read / list-clients (atomic SQL race-safe)

body:
## Summary

Write `sources.config` as real JSON on Postgres instead of
`JSON.stringify(...)+::jsonb`.

## Problem

Several source-management write paths persisted config through
`JSON.stringify(config)` into a `::jsonb` cast.

On Postgres this can store a JSON string shape instead of an object. Later
config merges then degrade into arrays, and object-path reads like
`config->>'last_full_cycle_at'` stop working.

This breaks doctor/autopilot freshness checks and corrupts source metadata.

## Fix

- add a safe Postgres config-write path using `sql.json(...)`
- use it in:
  - `sources` command config updates
  - `sources-ops` source creation paths
- preserve the existing PGLite behavior

## Tests

- extend Postgres parity coverage for `updateSourceConfig()`
- keep `sources-ops` coverage on add/create paths
- assert merged config stays a JSON object, not an array
title: fix(sources): stop double-encoding source config on Postgres

body:
## Summary

Adds a new config flag `auto_link_plain_text` (default: `false`) that enables automatic extraction of plain-text entity names from markdown content during `runAutoLink`.

## Problem

Currently, auto-link only creates graph edges for explicit `[[wikilinks]]`. Content that mentions people by plain text (e.g., "Patrick Skinner mentioned that...") doesn't get linked to their person pages, even when those pages exist.

## Solution

When `auto_link_plain_text = true`:
1. After extracting explicit wikilinks, the system runs `extractEntities()` on the markdown body
2. This uses a regex NER approach to find capitalized multi-word names (e.g., "Patrick Skinner", "John Chen")
3. For each candidate, it generates an expected slug via `slugifyEntity()` (e.g., `people/patrick-skinner`)
4. If the slug resolves to an existing page, a `mentions` edge is created with `link_source='plain_text'`
5. Dedupes against explicit wikilinks to avoid double-linking

## Changes

- `src/core/link-extraction.ts`: Added `isPlainTextEntityExtractionEnabled()` config helper
- `src/core/operations.ts`: Wire `extractEntities` into `runAutoLink`, add `plain_text` to reconcilable sources
- `test/link-extraction.test.ts`: 7 new tests for config flag behavior

## Testing

```bash
bun test test/link-extraction.test.ts
# 105/105 pass
```

## Usage

```bash
gbrain config set auto_link_plain_text true
gbrain sync --source <source> --full  # re-process existing pages
```

## Notes

- Opt-in by default to preserve backward compatibility
- The regex pattern `/\b([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)+)\b/g` catches most Western-style names
- Could be extended with more sophisticated NER in the future
title: feat: add opt-in plain-text entity extraction for auto-link

## Summary

- Create the admin dashboard Live Activity `EventSource` with `{ withCredentials: true }`.
- Rebuild the admin bundle and embedded asset manifest so compiled installs serve the fixed dashboard.
- Add a focused regression test that pins the credentialed `/admin/events` constructor.

## Why this matters

The dashboard already authenticates normal admin API calls with the `gbrain_admin` cookie, but the Live Activity panel uses a separate SSE connection. Behind reverse proxies, that request can arrive at `/admin/events` without the admin session cookie, which leaves the panel stuck on `connecting...` while the server correctly returns 401.

This keeps the server-side security model intact: `/admin/events` is still protected by the existing admin middleware. The client simply makes the SSE request match the authenticated dashboard session that loaded the page.

Fixes #912.

## Summary

- Add write-source and read-source controls to the admin **Register Agent** form.
- Add an authenticated `/admin/api/sources` endpoint so the UI can offer the same known source IDs the engine uses.
- Validate `source_id` and `federated_read` on the admin registration endpoint before creating the OAuth client.
- Surface source scope in the Agents table, drawer details, and JSON export.

## Why this matters

The admin dashboard is the obvious path for first-time operators creating MCP clients. Before this change, clients created there always landed on the `default` source with `federated_read = {default}`. On multi-source brains that makes the token exchange look successful while reads like `list_pages` and `query` return empty results for content stored in other sources.

This brings the admin path up to parity with the CLI registration path: an operator can create a client that writes to the intended source and reads across the selected federated sources without hand-editing `oauth_clients` afterward.

## Notes

- Existing default behavior is preserved: if no source fields are supplied, the admin endpoint uses `default` when available.
- API keys still show `all` in the UI because their existing bearer-token behavior is not changed.
- This does not try to solve the adjacent redirect URI editing gap from #1036.

Fixes #1490.

body:
## Summary

Fixes #1486.

The `postinstall` script used POSIX shell syntax (`command -v`, `>/dev/null 2>&1`, `1>&2`) which Bun's built-in script parser rejects on Windows. `bun install` aborts on Windows 11 with:

```
expected a command or assignment but got: "Redirect"
exit code 1
```

…before `gbrain` is even probed.

## Fix

Replace the POSIX one-liner with a `node -e` shim. `node` is a hard prerequisite of the install (bun is built on top of it on every platform, and `node` is documented in the existing setup) so we can rely on it here.

```js
const {spawnSync} = require('child_process');
const probe = spawnSync('gbrain', ['--version'], {stdio:'ignore', shell: process.platform === 'win32'});
if (probe.status === 0) {
  const r = spawnSync('gbrain', ['apply-migrations','--yes','--non-interactive'], {stdio:'inherit', shell: process.platform === 'win32'});
  process.exit(r.status || 0);
} else {
  process.stderr.write('[gbrain] postinstall skipped... See #218\\n');
}
```

- `shell: true` on Windows lets the spawn resolve `gbrain.cmd` shims (PATHEXT) correctly.
- Skip branch exits 0, so a fresh clone (where `gbrain` is not yet on `PATH`) still completes `bun install` exactly as today.
- POSIX hosts get identical end behavior: probe → migrate or skip.

## Risk

Lowest-risk surface — only the install probe changes. Behavior on macOS / Linux is preserved; Windows goes from hard-fail to working install.

## Test plan

```
$ node -e '<shim>'
[gbrain] postinstall skipped... See #218
$ echo $?
0
```

`package.json` parses (`JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(...))` ok). Cannot exercise the success branch without a built `gbrain` binary on PATH; that branch is the same `gbrain apply-migrations --yes --non-interactive` call as before, just spawned via Node instead of an inline shell pipe.

I do not have a Windows host to verify end-to-end; if you have CI for that, please rerun. Happy to iterate if there's a preferred shim style (cross-spawn, a real script under `scripts/`, etc.).
title: fix(postinstall): cross-platform node shim instead of POSIX shell

body:
## Summary

Fixes #1534.

`doctor`'s `stale_locks` check hard-coded `gbrain sync --break-lock` as the recovery hint for every stale row. But that command, per `sync.ts`, only handles `gbrain-sync:*` keys — it silently no-ops on `gbrain-cycle` (and per-source `gbrain-cycle:<source>`) locks with `Lock gbrain-sync:default is not held (nothing to break).`, leaving operators with no shipped path to clear the lock.

## Fix

Extracted a small `formatStaleLockBreakHint(lockId)` helper that branches on the lock-id prefix:

- `gbrain-sync:<src>` → `gbrain sync --break-lock --source <src>`
- `gbrain-sync` → `gbrain sync --break-lock`
- anything else (notably `gbrain-cycle`, `gbrain-cycle:<src>`, future kinds) → an honest SQL-fallback hint:  
  `manual clear required (no CLI breaker for <id>): DELETE FROM gbrain_cycle_locks WHERE id = '<id>';`

The `id` is single-quote-escaped so the printed snippet is safe to copy verbatim.

## Why this shape instead of shipping a new CLI breaker

The issue suggests also adding `gbrain dream --break-lock` or `gbrain admin break-lock <name>`. That's the right next step but it's a CLI-surface decision (PID liveness, force-break semantics, cross-host guards) that's better landed on its own with proper tests. This PR is the minimum honest-hint change so that `doctor` stops actively misleading operators today.

## Risk

Pure formatter change inside the doctor output builder. No DB writes, no new CLI surface, no behavior change for the existing `gbrain-sync:*` cases. Output text changes for cycle locks only.

## Test plan

No unit tests target this string today. Verified the helper output by extracting it into a Node REPL (covers sync, sync-with-source, cycle, cycle-with-source, and single-quote-in-id):

```
gbrain sync --break-lock --source default
gbrain sync --break-lock
manual clear required (no CLI breaker for gbrain-cycle): DELETE FROM gbrain_cycle_locks WHERE id = 'gbrain-cycle';
manual clear required (no CLI breaker for gbrain-cycle:notes): DELETE FROM gbrain_cycle_locks WHERE id = 'gbrain-cycle:notes';
manual clear required (no CLI breaker for weird's-lock): DELETE FROM gbrain_cycle_locks WHERE id = 'weird''s-lock';
```

body:
## Summary

- `gbrain autopilot status` silently started the daemon instead of reporting status. `runAutopilot()` only branched on flag forms, so positionals fell through to the default "start daemon" path.
- This PR adds a pure `translatePositionalSubcommands()` function that runs before any side effect (lockfile, daemon spawn, sync dispatch). Known positionals (`status`, `install`, `uninstall`, `start`) translate to their flag equivalents; unknown positionals fail loud with a structured error.
- Closes #1525.

## What's in the patch

`src/commands/autopilot.ts`:
- New exported pure function `translatePositionalSubcommands(args)` returning a discriminated `{ ok: true, args } | { ok: false, error }` so it's trivially testable without IO.
- Value-flag aware: `--repo /foo status` correctly treats `/foo` as the repo path and `status` as the positional, not vice versa. `--repo status` (literal path) passes through unchanged.
- Wired in `runAutopilot` right after the `--help` short-circuit and BEFORE the lockfile/daemon paths, so the fix-loud rejection happens before any side effect.
- `--help` text gains a "Subcommand aliases" block so the canonical flag forms are discoverable from the CLI itself, not just JSDoc.

`test/autopilot-positional-subcommands.test.ts` (new, 22 cases):
- Pure-function tests, no env mutation, no `mock.module` — passes `check-test-isolation.sh`.
- Iron-rule regression guard: first case is the exact ticket repro (`['status']` -> `['--status']`).

## Scope notes

- **`stop` is intentionally NOT aliased.** The ticket lists it as a potential alias, but stopping a running daemon is a new behavior (read PID from lock, send SIGTERM, await drain) that deserves its own design pass. Users typing `gbrain autopilot stop` today get the unknown-positional error rather than the silent daemon-start. Happy to land `stop` as a separate PR if there's interest.
- **Deprecation hints not added.** The ticket suggests a hint when positional form is used; I left it off because it's unclear whether the maintainer wants the positional form to eventually go away or stay supported indefinitely (consistent with modern CLI grammar). Easy to add in review if desired.
- **`--repo VALUE` and `--interval VALUE` only.** Those are the only value-flags in this command. Other autopilot flags are bare. If a future flag needs a value, add it to the `AUTOPILOT_VALUE_FLAGS` set.

## Test plan

- [x] `bun test test/autopilot-positional-subcommands.test.ts` — 22/22 pass
- [x] `bun test test/autopilot-*.test.ts` — all 109 existing autopilot tests pass (no regression)
- [x] `bun run typecheck` — clean
- [x] `bash scripts/check-test-isolation.sh` — clean (file passes R1/R2/R3/R4 isolation rules)
- [x] `bash scripts/check-jsonb-pattern.sh` — clean
- [x] `bash scripts/check-progress-to-stdout.sh` — clean
- [ ] Manual repro of the original bug pre-fix and post-fix:
  - `gbrain autopilot status` pre-fix → starts daemon (the ticket's bug)
  - `gbrain autopilot status` post-fix → routes to `showStatus()`, prints state, returns
- [ ] Manual `gbrain autopilot stop` post-fix → fails loud with "Unknown subcommand: \`stop\`" + list of allowed subcommands (does NOT start the daemon)

## Related

- Companion data-loss issue: #1526 (independent fix; targeting a separate PR after this one lands so each fix gets its own clean review surface).
- Family parent: #578 (partially fixed by #634 for the `--help` surface; this PR closes the unknown-positional surface).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: fix(autopilot): translate positional subcommands (closes #1525)

body:
## Why

`findByTitleFuzzy` on both `postgres-engine.ts` and `pglite-engine.ts` has no `source_id` filter and no `deleted_at IS NULL` filter. `tryFuzzyMatch` in `src/core/entities/resolve.ts` got both of those filters via #1436 (v0.41.13.0) for exactly the reasons that apply to its sibling here: fuzzy resolution can suggest cross-source slug candidates that the caller then silently drops at the FK filter (or worse, picks a soft-deleted page).

This is the missing twin of #1436. In multi-source brains, the live-mode auto-link resolver invoked from `put_page` (`operations.ts:937`) calls `engine.findByTitleFuzzy` with no scope. When two sources contain pages with similar titles (`people/alice-example` on `source-a`, `people/alice-other` on `source-b`), the fuzzy lookup can return the wrong-source slug, which then fails the downstream `allSlugs` / `addLink` FK filter — the link silently doesn't get created, and from the caller's view the resolver "failed" even though the page existed under the right source.

Reproducible with a 2-source PGLite setup + identical-title pages on both sides; the fuzzy call returns a slug whose `source_id` doesn't match the put_page caller's source.

## Impact

- 2-source brains: auto-links between same-title-different-source pages now resolve under the caller's source instead of the wrong neighbor.
- Soft-deleted pages can no longer be returned as fuzzy candidates (mirroring the resolve.ts fix from #1436).
- 1-source brains: no behavior change. `sourceId` is optional; when omitted the SQL takes the pre-existing unscoped path.

## Changes

- `engine.ts`: add optional 4th `sourceId` param to the `findByTitleFuzzy` interface + JSDoc explaining the scope semantics (live-mode callers should pass it; batch-mode callers like `gbrain extract` intentionally omit).
- `postgres-engine.ts` / `pglite-engine.ts`: implement the param via a conditional SQL branch that adds `AND source_id = $N AND deleted_at IS NULL` when `sourceId` is set; existing query path unchanged when omitted.
- `link-extraction.ts`: add optional `sourceId` to `makeResolver` opts, forward to `findByTitleFuzzy` in step 3 of the resolve chain.
- `operations.ts`: pass `opts?.sourceId` to `makeResolver` from the live-mode put_page resolver (the place that already knows the caller's source).

## Test plan

- New unit tests in `test/link-extraction.test.ts` (2 cases):
  - `opts.sourceId` is forwarded to `findByTitleFuzzy` when set.
  - `opts.sourceId` omitted → `findByTitleFuzzy` receives `undefined` (back-compat).
- `bun run typecheck` clean.
- `bun test test/link-extraction.test.ts test/entity-resolve.test.ts test/operations.test.ts test/extract.test.ts` — 145/145 pass.

## Follow-up

Codex flagged a coverage gap: no PGLite engine-level test exercising the actual SQL (the new tests verify the JS wire-up, not that the SQL with `sourceId` actually filters by `source_id` and excludes `deleted_at`). Happy to add a 5-row PGLite test in a follow-up if useful.
title: fix(entities): thread sourceId through findByTitleFuzzy + skip soft-deleted

body:
## Problem

The v0.41.1 wave shipped `src/commands/bench-publish.ts` (exporting `runBenchPublish`), documented `gbrain bench publish` in [`docs/eval-bench.md`](docs/eval-bench.md), and advertised the verb in [CHANGELOG.md v0.41.1.0](CHANGELOG.md), but `src/cli.ts` was never updated with the dispatcher case.

On master @ 32f8be96 (v0.41.14.0):

```bash
$ gbrain bench publish --from /tmp/captured.ndjson --to /tmp/baseline.ndjson
Unknown command: bench
Run gbrain --help for available commands.

$ grep "command === 'bench'" src/cli.ts
# (no matches)
```

The eval-loop documented in v0.41.1 CHANGELOG (`eval export → bench publish → eval gate`) is broken end-to-end on a fresh install.

## Fix

Two surgical edits to `src/cli.ts` mirroring the existing `case 'eval':` pattern:

1. Add `'bench'` to the `CLI_ONLY` set so the dispatcher reaches the case statement instead of rejecting at the cliOps lookup
2. Add a `case 'bench':` block that sub-dispatches `publish` to `runBenchPublish` (mirrors `case 'eval':` at cli.ts:1287-1290)

14 lines added, 1 deleted.

## Verification

Applied locally + tested on a v0.41.14.0 install:

```bash
$ gbrain bench publish
Error: --from FILE is required

gbrain bench publish — write a baseline file from captured queries
[full usage text follows...]

$ gbrain bench publish --from /tmp/captured.ndjson --to /tmp/baseline.ndjson --label test
Wrote /tmp/baseline.ndjson
  Label:    test
  Rows:     17
  [...]
$ gbrain eval gate --baseline /tmp/baseline.ndjson
[runs the regression gate to completion]
```

Closes #1474.

(Filed by Mr-B-1 — same v0.40.8 → v0.41.14 upgrade audit that produced sibling issues #1452-1455, #1475.)
title: fix(cli): wire gbrain bench publish dispatcher (closes #1474)

## Summary

`gpt-4o` and `gemini-1.5-pro` are no longer accepted by the gateway — `gpt-4o` was removed from the OpenAI recipe's chat allowlist and `gemini-1.5-pro` was retired by Google. This causes DEFAULT_SLOTS A and C to fail on every cross-modal-eval run, forcing an `INCONCLUSIVE` verdict even when the output is genuinely good.

This bumps the stale defaults in `src/core/cross-modal-eval/runner.ts` to current flagships from each provider's chat allowlist:

- Slot A: `openai:gpt-4o` → `openai:gpt-5.2` (only non-mini OpenAI chat model)
- Slot B: `anthropic:claude-opus-4-7` (unchanged — was working)
- Slot C: `google:gemini-1.5-pro` → `google:gemini-2.0-flash` (non-deprecated, non-experimental)

Also drops the stale `openai:gpt-4o` and `google:gemini-1.5-pro` entries from the `estimateCost()` PRICING map and adds entries for the new model IDs so cost estimates don't silently produce "no pricing on file" warnings.

## Test plan

- [ ] `bun run eval-cross-modal` against a known-good fixture — DEFAULT_SLOTS A and C return real verdicts instead of `INCONCLUSIVE`
- [ ] Cost estimate emits a real number for both A and C, no "no pricing on file" warning
- [ ] Slot B unchanged

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

body:
## Summary

New skill `transcribe` — concrete implementation of the video/audio branch of `media-ingest`. Local-first, free by default (`COST: $0.00`).

- **YouTube auto-captions fast path** (~2s) via `youtube-transcript-api`
- **Local fallback** via `yt-dlp` + `mlx-whisper` (~10 min/hr on Apple Silicon, 36× realtime)
- **Synthesis runs in the calling agent's session** — no extra API call
- **Hub-and-spoke filing with judgment**: always writes `originals/transcript-<slug>`; 0–N spokes based on `content_shape` (talk/tutorial/entity-feature/original-thinking), capped at 3
- **Iron-law back-linking** to every existing entity page touched

## Why

`media-ingest` is the high-level format router but ships no implementation. `transcribe` is the working pipeline for the video/audio rows of that table. PDFs/books/screenshots/repos remain in `media-ingest`.

Two skills, no overlap, one delegation arrow.

## What's in the PR

| File | What |
|---|---|
| `skills/transcribe/SKILL.md` | 7-phase contract (pre-flight → extract → synthesize → brain-first entity check → hub-and-spoke filing → back-links → summary). Frontmatter matches gbrain skill conventions. |
| `skills/transcribe/scripts/extract_transcript.py` | ~420-line orchestrator. CLI-callable. SHA-1 input cache, file-lock for GPU concurrency, exponential backoff retries, telemetry JSONL, structured exit codes (0/1/2/3). |
| `skills/transcribe/README.md` | Quick-start, dep install commands, platform notes. |
| `skills/manifest.json` | Registers `transcribe` between `media-ingest` and `meeting-ingestion`. |
| `skills/media-ingest/SKILL.md` | Phase 1 table delegates video/audio rows to `transcribe`. |

## Cache layout

`TRANSCRIBE_CACHE_DIR` > `$GBRAIN_HOME/transcribe` > `~/.gbrain/transcribe` (precedence).

## Platform support

- **Apple Silicon**: native `mlx-whisper`, 36× realtime
- **Other platforms**: orchestrator docstring documents the `faster-whisper` drop-in (same return shape, runs on CPU or CUDA)

## Cost contract

`COST: $0.00, payment: local + calling-agent subscription`. No paid transcription APIs wired in. If a future flag adds one, the skill MUST emit a `COST: ~$X.XX, payment: <source>` line and require user confirmation before the call — consistent with the brain-ops cost discipline.

## Tested

- YouTube with captions (~3.5 min Rick Astley): 2.9s end-to-end via `youtube-transcript-api`
- Same URL with `--force-whisper` (cold-start path including model download): 21s
- Cache hit on re-run: 0.0s
- Bad input path: exit code 1 with clear error
- Three real talks ran end-to-end via the full SKILL.md flow (Paul Graham Stockholm keynote, Tom Blomfield "Self-Improving Company" talk, Reiner Pope chip-design interview) — hub + spoke pages + back-link edges land correctly

## Departures from existing conventions (flagging for review)

- This is the first skill that ships its own auxiliary script (`scripts/extract_transcript.py`). Existing skills are SKILL.md-only. The orchestrator is callable via `python3 skills/transcribe/scripts/extract_transcript.py <url>` — if you'd rather inline it as a code block in SKILL.md or treat the script as out-of-tree, happy to refactor.
- Added a `README.md` at the skill root. Other skills don't have one. Drop it if you'd rather keep the convention.

## Test plan

- [ ] `python3 skills/transcribe/scripts/extract_transcript.py 'https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oIk3R-sMX5o'` returns a JSON object with `status: success`, `engine: youtube-transcript-api`, exit 0
- [ ] Re-run hits cache (`engine: cache`, `wall_time_seconds: 0.0`)
- [ ] Force whisper path with `--force-whisper --no-cache`, transcript returns
- [ ] `gbrain put originals/transcript-test < <hub>.md` upserts
- [ ] Back-link edges land via `gbrain link people/<author> originals/transcript-<slug> --type authored`

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: feat(skills): transcribe — video/audio → typed brain entries

body:
## Problem

A production brain (~16K pages, Supabase Postgres, custom schema pack) was upgraded from v0.29-era to v0.41.2. Post-upgrade audit found **3 critical bugs** and **8 features with no discoverability path** — operators can't activate what they can't find.

## Critical Bugs

### 1. Dream cycle loses DB connection on Supabase pooler (P0)

`gbrain dream` on a 16K-page brain fails for 13/16 phases:

```
  ✗ sync        No database connection: connect() has not been called.
  ✗ synthesize  No database connection: connect() has not been called.
  ✗ extract_facts  No database connection: connect() has not been called.
  ... (10 more phases)
```

The extract phase partially completes (scans 15,752 files) but loses ~381 timeline rows in late batches when the pooler drops the idle connection. All subsequent DB-dependent phases fail.

**Root cause:** Supabase PgBouncer (port 6543) evicts idle backends. The filesystem-scanning phases create long gaps with no DB queries. No reconnect logic between phases or on batch error.

### 2. `gbrain doctor` crashes on v0.41.2

```
$ gbrain doctor
undefined is not an object (evaluating 's.toLowerCase')
```

Worked on v0.41.0, broke after v0.41.2 upgrade. Likely null-safety issue in the new domain-aggregator code path when `calibration_domains` is empty/undeclared in the active schema pack.

### 3. Minion worker has no supervision or discoverability

A job has been `waiting` for 5 days with no worker to process it. Doctor's `queue_health` check reports "no stalled jobs" (technically true — it's waiting, not stalled). There's no:
- Doctor check for "jobs waiting but no worker running"
- `gbrain autopilot --install` option that includes the worker
- Default healthcheck/restart mechanism

## Feature Discoverability Gaps

An operator upgrading across 12 minor versions (v0.29 → v0.41) gets automatic schema migrations but **zero guidance** on new features to configure:

| Feature | Version | Requires | Discoverable? |
|---|---|---|---|
| Calibration domains | v0.41.2 | `calibration_domains` in pack YAML | ❌ No CLI, no docs, aggregator enum only in source |
| Dream cycle atom extraction | v0.41.2 | `phases:` field in custom pack | ❌ Not suggested by `schema review-candidates` |
| Nightly quality probe | v0.41.1 | Config flip | ❌ Doctor doesn't suggest |
| Eval gate / search baseline | v0.41.1 | `bench publish` + `eval gate` | ❌ No getting-started workflow |
| Code intelligence | v0.33-v0.34 | Code pages in brain | ❌ Commands exist but undocumented for operators |

Custom packs extending `gbrain-base` silently miss new pack-level features (`phases`, `calibration_domains`). No diff mechanism exists to show "gbrain-base now supports X that your pack doesn't declare."

## Proposed Solutions

### P0 — Fix broken things
1. **Connection resilience in cycle runner** — reconnect between phases; retry on batch insert error
2. **Null-safety in doctor's domain-aggregator path** — handle empty/missing `calibration_domains`

### P1 — Make doctor the upgrade advisor
3. Add `[SUGGEST]` tier to doctor output for feature activation hints:
   - "calibration_domains not declared — per-topic accuracy tracking available"
   - "nightly quality probe disabled — ~$10/mo for automatic regression detection"
   - "no eval baseline — `gbrain bench publish` protects search quality"
   - "jobs waiting but no worker detected"
   - "custom pack missing `phases` field — dream cycle won't run extract_atoms/synthesize_concepts"

### P2 — Upgrade experience
4. `gbrain upgrade --changelog` or `gbrain doctor --post-upgrade` — show what's new
5. `gbrain schema review-candidates` should suggest `phases` + `calibration_domains`
6. Document Supabase pooler idle timeout behavior and `GBRAIN_DISABLE_DIRECT_POOL`

## Full Audit

See [`docs/audits/v041-production-upgrade-audit.md`](docs/audits/v041-production-upgrade-audit.md) for the complete feature-by-feature analysis including environment details, error logs, and a prioritized action plan.

## Testing

- Audit performed on a live production brain
- Dream cycle failure reproduced on Supabase transaction pooler (port 6543)
- Doctor crash reproduced on fresh v0.41.2 install with custom pack (no calibration_domains)
title: docs: v0.29 to v0.41.2 production upgrade audit — 3 bugs + 8 feature gaps

body:
## Problem

doctor's `sync_freshness` check reads `sources.last_sync_at` to decide whether a source is stale. Two gaps:

**(A) No-op syncs never advanced `last_sync_at`.** When `performSync` returns early via the `up_to_date` path (git HEAD unchanged, chunker current, no detached working-tree changes), the column was never touched. So a quiet source (no upstream commits in N days) was misreported as "stale 16d" even though every cron tick polled it and got "Already up to date". The metric users intuit from "last sync" is *last time we attempted a sync*, not *last time we imported new data*.

**(B) `last_sync_at` advanced even when the git pull failed.** A network partition / revoked credential / diverged remote means we never observed remote state — but freshness still advanced, telling doctor/autopilot the source was healthy when it wasn't.

## Fix

- Advance `last_sync_at = now()` on the no-op `up_to_date` early-return (federated `opts.sourceId` path only; legacy `config.sync.last_run` path untouched).
- Gate every `last_commit` write site on `pullAttemptedAndFailed` (threaded through `writeSyncAnchor` + `performFullSync`): `last_commit`/`newest_content_at` still advance against the stale checkout, but the freshness signal is suppressed.
- Operator-skipped offline modes (`--no-pull`, detached HEAD, no origin) are NOT failures and DO advance. `--dry-run` stays side-effect free.

## Test

`test/sync-no-op-touches-last-sync-at.test.ts` (PGLite, 5 cases): no-op advances freshness; no-op without `sourceId` is a no-write; `--dry-run` mutates nothing; no-op + pull-failure does NOT advance; incremental + pull-failure advances `last_commit` but not `last_sync_at`. **5/5 green** on current master.

---
*Rebased onto current `master`. Upstream `#1794` (resumable incremental sync) reworked this path but covers **neither** gap — the no-op early-return at the `up_to_date` branch still skips the freshness write, and there's no pull-failure gate. Both halves of this fix still apply.*
title: fix(sync): record sync attempt timestamp on no-op + suppress on pull failure

body:
## Summary

\`gbrain models doctor\` silently falls through to the read view
instead of running the reachability probe.

\`runModels\` (src/commands/models.ts) checks
\`args[1] === 'doctor'\`, but the caller —
\`handleCliOnly(command, subArgs)\` in \`src/cli.ts:113\` — passes
\`subArgs\` (the leading command token already stripped). So inside
\`runModels\`, \`args[0]\` is the subcommand and \`args[1]\` is
undefined.

The doctor probe path has been unreachable from the CLI since the
\`handleCliOnly\` refactor. \`gbrain models help\` happens to work
by falling through to the \`--help\` flag detection
(\`args.includes('--help')\`), masking the bug.

Two-char fix: \`args[1]\` → \`args[0]\` on both branches of the
ternary.

## Verified

Pre-fix: \`gbrain models doctor\` prints the routing table (read
view) and exits 0 — probe never runs.

Post-fix on a real production brain:

\`\`\`
Model reachability probe:
  ✔ embedding_config  ollama:bge-m3                              ok (0ms)
  ✔ reranker_config   (none)                                     ok (0ms)
  ✘ chat              lmstudio:mistralai/magistral-small-2509    unknown (5012ms)
      [chat(lmstudio:mistralai/magistral-small-2509)] probe timed out after 5s
  ✔ expansion         lmstudio:google/gemma-4-e2b                ok (535ms)

Summary: 3/4 reachable.
\`\`\`

(The chat timeout in this snapshot is the user's LM Studio
having Magistral ejected, not a fix issue.)

## Tests

Manual regression — \`gbrain models doctor\` from a fresh checkout
prints the "Model reachability probe:" header. Unit test would
require mocking the gateway probe paths; happy to add in a
follow-up if a maintainer prefers a particular shape.

## Pre-push gate

\`bun run verify\` passes locally (privacy + jsonb + progress +
test-isolation + wasm + admin-build + typecheck).

## Trigger paths

Not retrieval-touching, so \`gbrain eval replay\` not required per
CONTRIBUTING.md.
title: fix(models): dispatch subcommand reads args[0] not args[1]

body:
## Summary

Add `SYNOPSIS_DOC_MAX_CHARS` env-overridable cap (default 32768 chars,
~8K tokens) applied in `buildUserPrompt` for the per-chunk synopsis
prompt. Truncates the TAIL of `documentText` so the head (title,
frontmatter, intro) carries the document-level anchor the synopsis
needs.

## Motivation

Small local chat models (Gemma 4 E2B, Qwen3 4B on Ollama / LM Studio)
get dramatically slower on long contexts even when their declared
window is 131K tokens. A 73K-char page synopsis on Gemma 4 E2B takes
60-120s, exceeding the worker's default 30s `lockDuration` and tripping
`lock-lost` errors during the contextual-retrieval per_chunk_synopsis
backfill.

This is a companion lever to issue #1014 / PR #1177 (`--lock-duration`
on `gbrain jobs work`) — bumping the lock TTL alone helps but lets each
job consume more wall-clock per chunk; capping `documentText` makes each
call complete *faster*, which lets the existing 30s lock survive on
medium-length pages too. Both together get the synopsis backfill running
reliably on small local LLMs without burning Anthropic credit.

## Behavior

- Default `SYNOPSIS_DOC_MAX_CHARS = 32768` (~8K tokens at 4 chars/tok)
- Env override: `GBRAIN_SYNOPSIS_DOC_MAX_CHARS=<int>` with range
  `[512, 1048576]` (clamped via regex; falls back to default on invalid)
- **Anthropic Haiku unaffected** at 32768 cap (prefill cheap, no
  per-chunk slowdown). Bump higher for frontier models if you want
  richer document anchoring.
- Truncation TAIL only — placeholder inserted: `[... N chars truncated
  for synopsis budget ...]`

## Verified

Real 1383-chunk personal brain backfill at:

\`\`\`
GBRAIN_SYNOPSIS_MODEL=lmstudio:google/gemma-4-e2b
GBRAIN_SYNOPSIS_DOC_MAX_CHARS=16384
gbrain jobs work --concurrency 4 --lock-duration 300000
\`\`\`

Pre-change: every transcript page (50-130 chunks each) hit \`lock-lost\` after ~30s. Throughput collapsed.

Post-change: no deaths, no stalls. Steady throughput at ~10 jobs/min during burst windows on a single LM Studio Gemma E2B instance.

## Tests

Manual regression: ran against the production-scale 1383-chunk
backfill described above. Unit test for the truncate behavior in
\`buildUserPrompt\` would require exporting the currently-private
helper or adding a test seam — happy to add in a follow-up if a
maintainer prefers a particular shape.

## Pre-push gate

\`bun run verify\` passes locally (privacy + jsonb + progress +
test-isolation + wasm + admin-build + typecheck).

## Trigger paths

Not retrieval-touching (no \`src/core/search/*\` changes), so
\`gbrain eval replay\` not required per CONTRIBUTING.md.

## Related

- Issue #1014 + PR #1177 — \`--lock-duration\` flag (the companion
  lever; this PR is the documentText half of the belt-and-suspenders
  pair)
- PR #1358 — synopsis model env override (independent; covers the
  AUTH side of the same backfill scenario)
title: feat(synopsis): tail-truncate documentText for small-model chat handlers

body:
## Summary

Three independent fixes to gbrain's import / embed path, motivated by a real-world incident where a cosmetic page-format change (added a Timeline section + HTML-comment footer to ~80K message pages) cascaded into ~99K wasted re-embeds over 24 hours, dominated by chunks whose semantic content was unchanged.

Each fix is a separate commit; each has its own test. Three commits, ~150 lines of source, three new test files.

## The bugs

### 1. Markdown chunks lose embeddings on position shifts

`importFromContent` re-embeds every chunk on a page when the page `content_hash` changes — even chunks whose text is byte-identical at a *different* position. The SQL upsert at \`pglite-engine.ts:1278-1284\` preserves embeddings only when \`chunk_text\` matches at the *same* \`(page_id, chunk_index)\`. Chunker overlap (\`recursive.ts:264-275\`) and \`splitBody\` rerouting routinely shift chunks between positions on any edit, defeating the SQL-level preservation.

The code-file path already has a position-keyed reuse map at \`import-file.ts:597-613\` — but the markdown path has nothing equivalent.

**Fix (c0ba5a9):** mirror the reuse pattern into \`importFromContent\`, keyed on \`chunk_text\` alone (not \`chunk_index:chunk_text\`). A chunk whose text appears at a different position still finds its embedding.

### 2. No \`--force\` on \`gbrain import\`

The \`existing.content_hash === hash\` short-circuit at \`import-file.ts:262\` can't be bypassed from the CLI. \`gbrain reindex --markdown\` threads \`forceRechunk\` but filters on \`chunker_version\`, so it skips pages whose chunker is current. Recovery of pages corrupted by partial imports (the incident: 78 pages stuck in our case, dedup cache desynced from page rows due to PGLite TOAST corruption) requires dropping to SQL: \`UPDATE pages SET content_hash = NULL WHERE slug IN (...)\`.

**Fix (bcc681d):** add \`--force\` to \`runImport\`; thread \`forceRechunk: true\` through to \`importFromContent\` and \`importImageFile\`. The opts type already accepted it; this just exposes it at the CLI surface.

### 3. Content hash includes presentation regions

The hash input at \`import-file.ts:241-250\` is computed over the entire \`compiled_truth\` and \`timeline\` strings. Renderers that emit auto-generated material (footers, summary tables, generated-at timestamps) can't update that material without invalidating the page hash and re-chunking the page.

**Fix (d53602a):** add a \`<!-- gbrain:no-hash-start --> ... <!-- gbrain:no-hash-end --></!\`> sentinel mechanism. Text inside the sentinels is stripped from the hash input only; it remains in the page body for chunking, search, and display. Combined with #1, a renderer can ship cosmetic updates with zero embedding cost.

## Tests

- \`test/import-file-embedding-reuse.test.ts\` — verifies an unchanged chunk's embedding survives a position shift. Uses a helper that runs a real first-import to learn the chunker's actual outputs, then mocks \`getChunks\` returning those exact texts at reversed positions.
- \`test/import-force-flag.test.ts\` — verifies \`forceRechunk: true\` bypasses the content_hash short-circuit on both \`importFile\` (markdown) and \`importImageFile\`.
- \`test/strip-no-hash-regions.test.ts\` — 6 unit tests for the regex helper (single region, multiple, multiline, whitespace tolerance, unmatched start sentinel).
- \`test/hash-sentinel-integration.test.ts\` — same content_hash before/after a sentinel-only edit; different content_hash when real content changes.

All four new test files pass. Existing import-file tests pass (modulo a pre-existing Windows symlink \`EPERM\` failure that fails on the baseline commit too).

## Impact

For users running gbrain against a markdown corpus with templated/generated material, this combination prevents per-renderer-tweak embedding cascades. In our incident, the same workflow would now write 0 chunks of new embedding work for a presentation-only update, vs. ~99K.

The CLI's behavior surface gains \`--force\`. The hash input changes only in the presence of the sentinel comments — backwards-compatible for any page that doesn't use them.

## Local provenance

Pushed from \`defenestrate2/gbrain:fix/embedding-reuse-and-force\` off upstream \`baf1a47\` (v0.35.0.0). Used locally for a few days against a ~77K-page brain; \`--force\` recovered 78 stuck pages in 7.5s, body-keyed reuse preserved embeddings for the rest.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: fix(import): body-keyed embedding reuse + --force + no-hash sentinel

body:
## Summary

**`gbrain extract links --source fs` silently drops every outbound wikilink from any file named `index.md`, `log.md`, `README.md`, or `schema.md`** — at every depth in the vault. The walker calls `isSyncable()`, which hard-skips those four basenames. But `gbrain import` *has* created those files as pages via a separate code path. The result is asymmetric: those pages exist in `pages`, get embedded, show up in search — but their outbound edges never make it into the `links` table. They're zombie pages.

This bites hardest when those meta-files are the actual hubs of the brain rather than project boilerplate. On the brain I'm running, `vault/index.md` carries ~70 wikilinks (equipo, productos, clientes, fuentes tables) and `vault/data_room_pre-seed/*/README.md` is the entry point for nine sub-folders. Every one of those was orphan in the graph despite being visibly central in the content.

## Repro

```bash
mkdir -p /tmp/v/wiki
printf '# hub\n- [[wiki/foo]]\n' > /tmp/v/index.md
printf '# foo\n' > /tmp/v/wiki/foo.md

gbrain import /tmp/v
gbrain extract links --source fs --dir /tmp/v
gbrain graph index --depth 1
# → links: []   (expected: 1 → wiki/foo)
```

Same outcome for `log.md`, any `README.md`, or `schema.md`.

## Fix

Add `skipMetaFiles?: boolean` to `SyncableOptions` (default `true` preserves the current remote-sync behavior — `gbrain sync` callers in `commands/sync.ts` should keep skipping these basenames so project boilerplate isn't pushed). Pass `false` from the shared `walkMarkdownFiles` in `commands/extract.ts` so the local link harvester treats them as the pages they really are.

No call-site changes required for downstream consumers — they continue to get the default-true behavior. The flag is purely additive.

## Why opt-in rather than removing the skip entirely

The skip is correct for `gbrain sync`'s push-to-remote path: you don't want every project's `README.md` and `index.md` pushed to a shared sync target as if they were content pages. The skip is wrong only for *local* walkers that treat the brain dir as the source of truth and need every page's edges. `skipMetaFiles: false` lets each caller declare its own intent.

## Observed impact

305-page personal brain, single `extract links --source fs` run after the patch:

| metric | before | after |
|---|---|---|
| pages_processed | 267 | **280** (+13 meta-files) |
| links_created (one run) | 0 (idempotent) | **436** |
| outbound from `index` | 18 (manual `gbrain link`) | **243** |
| outbound from `log` | 0 | **118** |
| orphans | 26 | **10** (remaining are bitácora timestamp leaves) |
| brain_score | 84 | 84 (orphans 14/15, link coverage entities 100%) |

## Test plan

- [x] `bun run typecheck` — no new errors in `src/` (pre-existing fuzz / chokidar / js-yaml issues unrelated)
- [x] End-to-end on a real 305-page brain: import → extract → graph queries show outbound from `index.md` / `log.md` / data-room READMEs (previously all 0)
- [x] Sanity-checked that `gbrain sync` path is unaffected (only `extract.ts` walker opts in)
- [ ] Maintainer to confirm no other call-site should also opt in (frontmatter command? brain-writer? would value a second look)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: fix(extract): opt-in skipMetaFiles so meta-file wikilinks get harvested

body:
## Problem

`gbrain lint --fix` corrupts any page that contains a ` ```markdown ` (or ` ```md `) fenced block **mid-document**: it strips the block's **closing** fence, leaving an unbalanced/open fence. Because `gbrain autopilot` runs `lint --fix` every cycle, affected notes are re-corrupted continuously — reverting by hand never sticks.

Real-world trigger: Notion-exported notes that fence a config/snippet block partway down the page. Observed on a vault where the autopilot re-stripped the same notes every ~150s cycle.

## Root cause

Detection and the fixer were out of sync in `src/commands/lint.ts`.

**Detection** (`code-fence-wrap` rule) used `/m`-flagged regexes — fires when a ` ```markdown ` opener starts *any line* **and** a closing ` ``` ` ends *any line*:

```ts
if (content.match(/^```(?:markdown|md)\s*\n/m) && content.match(/\n```\s*$/m)) { … }
```

**Fixer** (`fixContent`) used **string-anchored** regexes (no `/m`):

```ts
fixed = fixed.replace(/^```(?:markdown|md)\s*\n/, ''); // only matches at byte 0
fixed = fixed.replace(/\n```\s*$/, '');                // only matches at EOF
```

For a page whose ` ```markdown ` block is **not** at byte 0 (e.g. after frontmatter and prose), the opener-strip no-ops while the closer-strip still fires at EOF — so only the closing fence is removed, and the rule re-flags + re-mangles forever.

## Fix

Detection and fixer now share one `analyzeMarkdownWrap()` helper that treats a page as fence-wrapped **only when the entire body (after frontmatter) is a single fence**: the first non-blank body line opens it, the last non-blank line closes it, and no bare ` ``` ` appears between them (which would mean the fence closed early). A mid-document fenced block is left untouched. Routing both the rule and the fixer through one code path keeps them from drifting apart again.

## Tests

`bun test test/lint.test.ts` → **20 pass**. Two regressions added:

- `lintContent` does **not** flag a mid-document ` ```markdown ` block as a page wrap.
- `fixContent` leaves such a block intact — closing fence preserved, fences balanced.

Existing whole-page-wrap detection/fix tests still pass; `tsc --noEmit` is clean.

---
🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: fix(lint): only unwrap whole-page code fences, not mid-document blocks

body:
## Summary

The HTTP MCP server's 401 responses miss the `resource_metadata` parameter that the MCP authorization spec (2025-06-18 draft §5.1) and RFC 9728 require. MCP-aware OAuth clients (claude.ai, Cursor, etc.) use that URL to find the authorization-server discovery doc + /token + /authorize without the user manually pasting URLs.

## Repro

1. Start gbrain serve --http with --enable-dcr behind a public URL (Tailscale Funnel, ngrok, Cloudflare tunnel, etc.)
2. Open claude.ai → Settings → Connectors → Add custom connector
3. Enter the MCP URL (e.g. `https://your-host:8443/mcp`)
4. claude.ai shows "Couldn't reach the MCP server" and never starts the OAuth redirect.

Pre-fix the relevant 401 looked like:

```
HTTP/2 401
www-authenticate: Bearer error="invalid_token", error_description="Missing Authorization header"
```

MCP-aware clients can't begin the OAuth handshake from that header because there's nothing telling them where the resource metadata lives. Discovery, /token, and /register all individually responded 200 in my tests — but claude.ai couldn't find them from the 401 envelope.

## Fix

Compute the resource-metadata URL once near the issuer URL setup and pass it into both `requireBearerAuth` middleware call sites (`/mcp` and `/ingest`). The `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` `BearerAuthMiddlewareOptions` already supports a `resourceMetadataUrl` parameter and emits the correct header shape:

```
www-authenticate: Bearer error="invalid_token",
                  error_description="Missing Authorization header",
                  resource_metadata="https://your-host:8443/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource"
```

## Verification

- `bun run typecheck` clean
- Live curl against `https://...:8443/mcp` confirms the new header includes `resource_metadata`
- Real claude.ai connector attempt: pre-fix showed "Couldn't reach the MCP server" with no OAuth redirect; post-fix the connector begins the authorization flow normally.

## References

- MCP authorization spec (2025-06-18 draft §5.1): https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/draft/basic/authorization
- RFC 9728 (OAuth 2.0 Protected Resource Metadata): https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9728

## Test plan

- [x] Typecheck clean
- [x] Manual probe of WWW-Authenticate header via curl
- [x] Manual claude.ai custom-connector add (pre-fix fail, post-fix pass)
title: fix(serve-http): add resource_metadata to WWW-Authenticate per MCP spec + RFC 9728
